nd 27 - D - 5 Multiple wounds 27 - B - 3 Serious haemorrhage

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 132 SOP 3.6 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.6 HIGH RISK PREGNA NCY COMPLICATIONS Implementation Date 25 February 2020 Purpose To handle all pregnancy, childbirth, and miscarriage cases by following the MPDS protocol and local procedures approved by HMCAS Medical Director. Responsibility ALL HMCAS COMMUNICAT ION STAFF, SPECIFICA LLY CALL TAKERS 1. High - risk complications (pregnancies) are defined as conditions that place the mother, fetus, or both at higher - than - normal risk to complications during or after the pregnancy and birth. 1.1 Provide pre - arrival instructions as indicated by the patient’s condition and the DLS links in the MPDS protocols. 1.2 For prem ature or multiple births, provide delivery instructions whenever possible and safe to give. 1.3 Keep the caller on the line for emotional support, and give exit instructions, if pre - arrival delivery instructions are not necessary. 2. All factors listed below are considered high risk complications during pregnancy and any time the caller reports one or more of the be

low conditions, the call taker shall select the determinant descriptor “HIGH RISK complications” and code the case as a 24 - D - 5 (only if higher code s are not applicable). 2.1 High Risk Complications: 2.1.1 Premature birth (24 - 36 weeks) 2.1.2 Multiple birth (equal or greater to 24 weeks) 2.1.3 Bleeding Disorder 2.1.4 Blood Thinners 2.1.5 Cervical cerclage (stitch) 2.1.6 Placenta abruption 2.1.7 Placenta Previa 2.1.8 FEMALE GENITAL MUTILATION 2.2 Maternal fa ctors: (HMCAS factors) : 2.2.1 Age (younger than age 15, older than age 35) 2.2.2 Weight (Pre - pregnancy weight under 45.35 Kg or obesity) 2.2.3 Height (under 152.4cm/1.5m) 2.2.4 Previous pregnancy complications (stillbirth, fetal loss, preterm labor and/or delivery, small - for - ge stational age baby, large baby, seizure, pre - eclampsia, or eclampsia). 2.2.5 More than five previous pregnancies 2.2.6 Bleeding during the third trimester 2.2.7 Abnormalities of the reproductive tract 2.2.8 Uterine fibroids 2.2.9 Hypertension 2.2.10 Rh incompatibility

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 133 2.2.11 Gestational diabetics 2.2.12 An y infection 2.2.13 Fever 2.2.14 Acute surgical emergency (appendicitis, gallbladder disease, bowel obstruction). 2.2.15 Post - term pregnancy 2.2.16 Pre - existing chronic illness (such as asthma, autoimmune disease, cancer, sickle cell anemia, tuberculosis, herpes, AIDS, heart disease, kidney disease, Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, diabetes). 2.3 Fetal factors include : 2.3.1 Exposure to infection (especially herpes simplex, viral hepatitis, mumps, rubella, varicella, syphilis, toxoplasmosis, and infection caused by Coxsackievirus). 2.3.2 Exposure to damaging medications (especially phenytoin, folic acid antagonists, lithium, streptomycin, tetracycline, thalidomide, and warfarin); exposure to addictive substances (cigarette smoking, alcohol intake and illicit or abused drugs). 2.3.3 Prenatal test s indicating a serious health problem (for example, a heart defect ).

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 134 SOP 3.7 .A Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.7 .A THE EMERGENCY RULE Implementation Date 01 October 2016 Purpose To ensure that EMDs complete the minimum MPDS requirements for a safe and effective response during situations of extreme system overload. Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS S TAFF 1. In situations of extreme over - capacity in the emergency call center, the EMD may suspend Pre - Arrival Instructions (PAIs) and suspend or discontinue Post - Dispatch Instructions (PDIs) once Case Entry, Key Questions and final coding are completed. 2. If there are unanswered emer gency calls waiting in the 999 queue, the EMD shall make every effort to answer those calls ensuring that any current case(s) being processed are given a Final Determinant Code and specific responder crews are notified (or will be imminently notified by a co - worker). 3. Emergency Rule Situations: 3.1 Make every attempt to complete the interrogation sequence. 3.2 At a minimum, correctly obtain the Case Entry and Key Question information, and assign a final De

terminant Coding. 3.3 The following are the acceptable reasons for call takers to suspend or discontinue the MPDS protocol: 3.3.1 All call takers are already busy taking emergency calls, with one or more calls in queue (waiting to be answered). 3.3.2 Mass Casualty Incidents (where both phone and radio traffic are extreme) . 3.3.3 When instructed by shift supervisor/Oscar NCC. 3.4 Release the caller with the following statement (or similar) … “I need to hang up now to take another call). Help is on the way. If he/she gets worse in any way, call me back immediately for further instruct ions.” Also, whenever appropriate, say “If he/she becomes less awake and vomits, quickly turn him/her on his/her side.” 3.5 Notify the shift Supervisor 3.6 Document in ProQA “free text” comments field that Emergency rule applied. 3.7 If the call taker used manual MPDS flipchart sets, the comment “ Emergency rule applied” will be logged in the CAD. 3.8 Once the peak period is over and when possible and appropriate the call taker will make call back attempts to continue the interrogation and provide instructions. 4. Tracking of Emergency Rule Cases: 4.1 The EMD call taker will record all incidents where
he/she applies the Emergency Rule. 4.2 The supervisor will document in the shift daily report the time when the emergency rule was applied and the trip number of the cases when applicable .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 135 SOP 3.8 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.8 DISPATCHING WITH MOBILE DATA TERMINA LS Implementation Date 01 October 2016 Purpose To ensure efficient workflow during dispatch via Mobile Data Terminals . Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS S TAFF 1. The Dispatcher will verify the MDT signal in NJM: (This will be repeated each time the MDT goes offline ) . 1.1 If the indicator is RED , the Dispatcher shall report “ Negative .” The Responder should then log off, log on again, and repeat the confirmation again with the dispatcher. 1.2 If the indicator is GREEN , the Dispatcher shall report “ Affirmative. ” 2. The dispatcher shall call the unit via the radio channel and advise about the incoming P1 or P2 call. If the crew does not get the information on the MDT, they should revert to the emergency procedure. 3. The unit once received the call on MDT should accept the CFS and confirm w ith the dispatcher immediately. 4. If the unit has an active MDT, the responder should receive the call via the radio and simultaneously accept it through

the MDT. All other updates of the unit status will be done ONLY via MDT, EXCEPT when: 4.1 Calling for addit ional help 4.2 With Patient 4.3 Reporting a safety issue 4.4 MDT is offline or when there is MDT failure 4.5 In emergency (Requesting for specific urgent assistance, reporting the number of victims, MCI, and any status not available in the MDT). 5. At the end of the call and when becoming available, the responder will update through MDT with “ CLEARED ” status: 5.1 If the unit is already available for service, then they have reported “Available” via the radio and the dispatcher has changed the unit from NJM as “ Assigned Available. ” 5.2 If the unit is NOT available for service for any reason and the Dispatcher advised them to change to Standby channel and the PTL will update their unit status to Unavailable status. 6. Each dispatcher should monitor meticulously the update of UNIT STATUS do ne by responders and follow up the accuracy with AVL updates. 7. Each dispatcher should monitor the connectivity of MDTs to NJM network and report failure to the responding unit and to the PTL. 8. The PTL should keep an updated record of all units with Active MD T, updates any MDT change

as required, and notify the DTL and Oscar on duty with the list of units with Active MDT. 9. MDT ticketing and change from vehicle to vehicle will be the responsibility of Production/Vehicle - Control Supervisors. 10. Production/Vehicle - Co ntrol Supervisors should immediately notify the PTL any time MDT is changed from vehicle to another .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 136 SOP 3.9 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.9 DAILY OPERATIONS IN T HE COMMUNICATIONS CE NTRES Implementation Date 09 March 2020 Purpose To ensure staff follow appropriate daily shift procedures . Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS S TAFF 1. All staff shall familiarize him or herself with, and follow all rules, regulations, policies, and directives of the HMCAS Communications Centres (which include HMC and MOI). 2. Professionalism: 2.1 Staff shall conduct themselves in a professional manner as described in OP 4036 (HMC Code of Conduct) and AS 9035 (Dre ss Code and Personal Hygiene). 2.1.1 Staff will be courteous, kind, patient, and respectful in dealing with the public, field staff and peers. 2.1.2 Therefore, (for example) staff will refrain from the following behaviours, not limited to: 2.1.2.1 Loud talking, boisterous laughter and rough play . 2.1.2.2 Threatening, abusive or insulting/profane language . 2.1.2.3 Improper, insubordinate, or argumentative actions or comments . 2.1.2.4 Publicly criticize, disrespect, or ridicule the

service or the corporation . 2.1.2.5 Knowingly make any false stat ements or misrepresentation of facts . 2.1.2.6 Use service or corporation equipment, supplies and vehicles for personal use. 2.1.3 Staff shall not report for duty under the influence of any controlled substance, narcotic, or hallucinogens to the extent that it impairs th eir performance of duty in compliance with HR 3001(Alcohol and Drugs Abuse) . 2.1.4 There shall be no use of any tobacco products (smoking or chewing) in compliance with SA 1052 (Anti - Smoking). 2.2 Communications Centre information must be kept confidential and not d iscussed with any other party unless expressed permission is received from the Operations Manager. 3. Reporting for duty: 3.1 Staff should be well rested and prepared to perform his/her duties. 3.1.1 Staff are required to attend work fit to safely perform their duties without risk to themselves or others. 3.1.1.1 Staff should manage this personally by ensuring that an individual’s time away from work includes sufficient sleep and recovery. 3.1.1.1.1 This will include amongst other things, staff managing any health, lifestyle, personal or family responsibilities that may impact on the

ir fatigue (loss of alertness). 3.1.1.1.1.1 Staff experiencing fatigue should identify the factors that are contributing to fatigue, discuss any issues with their immediate supervisor, make changes as required, including sleeping

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 137 patterns, workloads, rosters, and lifestyle behaviors and seek professional help if necessary. 3.1.1.1.1.2 Supervisors should be alert for signs of drowsiness on the job and insist that an in - shift break be taken when these are observed. 3.1.1.1.1.3 Furthermore, sleeping on duty shall be cause of disciplinary action. 3.2 Staff are expected to report for duty as scheduled and t o notify the immediate Supervisor in a timely manner whenever they are unable to do so as described in HR 3033 9 (Punctuality) . 3.2.1 If the staff expects to be late, she/he should contact the duty Supervisor as soon as possible on the day when she/he will be la te. 3.2.1.1 This notification is required no later than one hour before the scheduled start of his/her shift. 3.2.2 Staff shall not fail to arrive at work at, or depart from work prior to, the designated time without prior approval, which includes arriving late at the b eginning of the duty, returning late from breaks, or an early departure from work. 3.2.3 Failure to report to work on time (being ‘tardy ’) shall be a cause of disciplinary action. 3.3 Staff shall report timeously for

duty with sufficient time to receive their assignment, receive a proper endorsement and be prepared to assume responsibility of their position at the scheduled start time. 3.3.1 The Operations Officer has the discretion to assign Team Leaders to certain roles (for example, Production, Distribution, Call - Taker, Dispatcher or Case Controller, etc.) based on the structure of the team, the expertise of the staff. 3.3.2 The Team Leader has the discretion to assign staff to certain roles (for example, Call - Taker, Dispatcher or Case Controller, etc.) based on the stru cture of the team, the expertise of the staff. 3.3.3 Reporting for duty activities include but are not limited to the following: 3.3.3.1 Be in uniform in accordance with AS 9035 3.3.3.2 Sign in using time/attendance system 3.3.3.3 Staff must sign in with their HMC account followed on t he CAD system . 3.3.3.4 Check HMC email for communications memo/policy/procedures notifications and updates. 3.3.3.5 Assure equipment is in working order (telephone, radio, and computer, etc. ) and do Microsoft Teams consoles checklist at the beginning of each shift . 3.3.3.6 Assure the console is clean and tidy 3.3.4 Staff shall report an

y needs/problems to their immediate supervisor. 4. Going off duty: 4.1 Staff shall be responsible for assuring that the on - coming shift receives a proper endorsement and is knowledgeable of issues that will carry over. Going off duty activities include but are not limited to: 4.1.1 Review and endorse on - going calls and pending cases .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 138 4.1.2 Review and endorse unit availability and HUB/spoke coverage . 4.1.3 Review and endorse event or specialized unit coverage . 4.1.4 Clean and tidy th e console 4.1.5 Sign out using time/attendance system 4.2 The Operations Officer and/or Team Leader has the discretion to keep staff on duty beyond their scheduled end of shift based on operational need (as per the accountabilities in the job description). 5. Staff bre aks: 5.1 Staff breaks are encouraged and should be taken/scheduled as approved by the Team Leader. 5.2 Staff working in the control rooms will take one break every 2 - hours. 5.2.1 The duration of the breaks is 30 min . 5.2.2 10 min for Prayer (does not count as a regular ‘break ’) . 5.3 Breaks should not be taken within the first hour or the last hour of the staff’s shift. 5.4 Breaks should not be combined. 5.5 Breaks are subject to recall/change/shortening depending on the workload at any time by the Team Leader . 5.6 Food and beverages should on ly be consumed on breaks, outside of the control rooms and in designated areas. 5.6.1 Water is permitted in the control rooms, but they should be in a spill

proof container and placed so as to avoid spills on the console/equipment. 6. Personal communications device s: 6.1 The use of any personal communication device ( smart - phones, tablets, computers, etc.) during daily operations prohibited in the control rooms. 6.2 Personal phone calls can be made during breaks and the duration of the phone call should not exceed the durati on of the break.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 139 SOP 3.10 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.10 TELEPHONE SYSTE M FAILURE Implementation Date 21 September 2021 Purpose To ensure staff follow appropriate procedures in the case of a telephone system failure . Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS ( HCS) STAFF 1. If the 999 system fails: 1.1 Immediately notify: 1.1.1 The MOI via established trouble reporting number: 44526 - 520 or 2345 - 520 . 1.1.2 If the above number was busy call 44526751or 2345751. Write down the shutdown timing and take the technician's name. 1.1.3 Ooredoo hotline via established trouble reporting number: 44526 - 52 0 or 2345 - 520. 1.1.4 The on - call Communications Manager (Silver Communications). 2. In case of Telephone System Failure : 2.1 Calls will be routed to the basic PBX backup phone sets located on each console. 2.2 Calls may be transferred from the backup phones by using the ‘f lash’ key. 2.3 There will be no speed dial capability and no ANI/ALI display. 2.4 A determination will be made by the on - call Communications Manager (Silver Communications)

about whether there is still enough functional equipment at the NCC or if there is need to evacuate. 2.5 If fifteen (15) minutes has passed after the termination of 999 services and service has not been restored, the following notifications will be made along with any other notifications requested by the on - call Communications Manager (Silver Commun ications) 2.5.1 Notify Operations Managers, Senior Operations Managers, Directors, and the Executive on call for AS ES and HCS. 2.6 If the outage cannot be resolved quickly and the decision to move to the Backup Centre has been made, follow the Evacuation Plan to re quest re - routing of calls (to the PTS call center). 2.7 All reported equipment failures must be documented by the Supervisor or Senior Officer on duty via the “OVA” reporting system as per HMC policy. 3. In case of General GSM/Ooredoo shutdown: 3.1 Check if it is general for land line phone and Vodafone GSM. 3.2 Check if the “LIFENET” function or not 3.3 Check the MDT functionality 3.4 Check the AVL functionality 3.5 Check the HIA call center 3.6 Check the internet, Microsoft team, Trust case, WhatsApp…etc. 3.7 Do radio check and keep the unit’s crew standby inside the A
mbulance. 3.8 All reported equipment failures must be documented by the Supervisor or Senior Officer on duty via the “OVA” reporting system as per HMC policy.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 140 4. If the HCC telephone system fails : 4.1 Immediately notify: 4.1.1 HMC Communica tions Department at 44392210 , 66262083 on call technicians. 4.1.1.1 Failure to reach any on those numbers contact 40260334 (bleep station) and ask them to contact the technician. 4.1.2 On duty Communications Centre Supervisor or Operation Officer. 4.1.3 The on - call Communicat ions Manager (Silver Communications). 4.1.4 Request HCC staff at NCC log into HCC Cisco workstations and take calls. 4.2 If fifteen (15) minutes has passed after the termination of HCC telephone system and service has not been restored, the following notifications w ill be made along with any other notifications requested by the on - call Communications Manager (Silver Communications). 4.2.1 Notify the SAS and MHS on - call managers (or any other users like HHCS and Diabetic). 4.2.2 If the issue is Cisco related, the on - call Communic ations Manager (Silver Communications) will request re - routing of calls to the DID numbers (direct HCC extensions) hunt Group 28201, 28202, 28203, 28204, 28205, 28207. 4.2.3 If the issue is not Cisco related, the on - call Commu

nications Manager (Silver Communicat ions) will request Communications to re - route calls to the emergency message: 4.2.3.1 Thank you for calling the Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service. At this time, we are experiencing technical difficulties and we are unable to attend to your call. We are w orking to rectify the problem. If this is a life - threatening emergency, please hang up and dial 999. For all other queries, please hang up and dial 40002055, 40442555. 4.3 If the outage cannot be resolved quickly and the decision to move to the Backup Centre has been made, follow the Evacuation Plan 4.4 All reported equipment failures must be documented by the on - duty Communications Centre Supervisor or Operation Officer via th e “OVA” reporting system as per HMC policy and complete a summary report, check list for telephone shut down. 5. If the Clinical Desk telephone system fails: 5.1 Immediately contact HMC Communications Department on - call technician at 44392210 , 66262083 . 5.1.1 If unable to successfully contact any of these numbers, contact 40260334 (bleep station) and ask them to contact the technician. 5.1.2 Request that all incoming telephone calls from the 98833 IVR

are forwarded to the direct dial extension hunt group 28251, 28252, 28253, 28254, 28255. 5.2 In addition to 5.1 above immediately notify: 5.2.1 Operations Supervisor NCC (Oscar NCC) 5.2.2 Operations Supervisor for HCC (Oscar HCC) 5.2.3 The on - call Communications Manager (Silver Communications) 5.2.4 Critical Care Services Manager on - call (CCS Manager)

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 141 5.3 If un able to make outbound calls, ICCS or the NCC Cisco phone should be used to make outbound calls. 5.4 If fifteen (15) minutes has passed after the termination of Clinical Desk telephone system and service has not been restored, the following notifications will b e made along with any other notifications requested by the CCS Manager on - call or Silver Communications: 5.4.1 Senior Consultant CCP: Clinical Desk 5.4.2 Consultant CCP: Effectiveness 5.4.3 Oscar Retrieval 5.4.4 Oscar LifeFlight 5.4.5 Senior Operations Manager: Scheduled Ambulance Serv ices 5.4.6 Senior Operations Manager: Emergency Ambulance Services 5.5 The system failure should be documented in the shift reports and an OVA created by the CC: Retrieval or CC: Emergency as per HMC policy. 6. Testing : 6.1 If there have been no failures of the system, the failover system will be tested and documented bi - annually .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 142 SOP 3.11 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3. 11 RADIO SYSTEM FAIL URE Implementation Date 09 March 2020 Purpose To ensure staff follow appropriate procedures in the case of a TETRA radio system failure in NCC and/or HCC . Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS S TAFF 1. Management of a Radio System Failure at National Command Center (NCC ) : 1.1 If a problem with any facet of the radio system arises, 1.1.1 The shift supervisor or the most senior person (staff) will contact NCC Maintenance Team via internal NCC extension line 44526520 or 44526751 and report the problem. 1.1.2 If unsuccess ful, the shift supervisor will notify the NCC - MOI Operations Officer (via 45444 or 45333) and escalate the issue to the Communications Operations Manager. 1.1.3 In the event of radio consoles failure, dispatchers will use the backup TETRA Mounted Radios (TMR) lo cated beneath each radio console when available; otherwise the TETRA Handheld Radios (THR) will be used with the existing control channels. 1.2 Complete Radio System Failure at NCC : 1.2.1

In case of a radio system failure which makes the TETRA radios (mounted and mo bile) out of service at NCC the following steps shall be followed: 1.2.1.1 Switch radio operations to a backup radio system if provided by Ministry of Interior (MOI). 1.2.1.2 Contact the radio workshop hotline 2341225 immediately. 1.2.1.3 Notify Operations Managers, Senior Operat ions Managers, Directors, and the Executive on call of AS ES, SAS and HCS. 1.2.1.4 If radio communications still cannot be made, each dispatcher will contact each unit through individual crew’s mobile phones, advise them to update their unit status, and await furt her instructions. 1.2.1.5 Notify the Oscar on duty via mobile phone. 1.2.1.6 The on - call Communications Manager will report to the Communications Center. 1.2.1.7 The telephone system will become the primary dispatch system for HMCAS and where necessary, some units shall be supplied with a cellular phone (provided by the production supervisor of each hub). 1.2.1.8 Record all relevant cellular phone numbers accurately in the resource management application and make available to all communications staff, supervisors, and managers. 1.2.1.9 Upon restoration of the Radio S

ystem's functionality, the on - duty Oscar or the Duty Team Leader (DTL) is responsible for verifying the operational status of the system. This is achieved by directing each dispatcher across all channels to conduct a radio check with all respective units, ensuring comprehensive system functionality.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 143 2. Management of Radio failure at Healthcare Coordination Center (HCC): 2.1 In case of radio system failure which renders the TETRA system out of service the following steps will take place: 2.1.1 PTL should verify that it is not a user or individual radio issue. 2.1.1.1 Contact Oscar - NCC to determine of NCC is experiencing any issues. 2.1.2 Switch radio operations to an assigned backup radio system if provided by MOI. 2.1.3 Contact the radio workshop immediatel y. ( Hotline: 2341225). 2.1.4 Notify Operations Managers, Senior Operations Managers, Directors and the Executive on call AS ES, SAS and HCS. 2.1.5 If radio communications still cannot be made, each dispatcher will contact each unit through individual crew’s mobile pho nes, advise them to update their unit status, and await further instructions. 2.1.6 The on - call Communications Manager will report to the Communications Center. 2.1.7 The telephone system will become the primary dispatch system for HMCAS and where necessary, some unit s shall be supplied with a cellular telephone (provided by the HMCAS if available). 2.1.8 Record all relevant cellular teleph

one numbers accurately in the resource management application of the CAD and make it available to all communications staff, supervisors, and managers. 3. Documentation: 3.1 All reported equipment failures must be documented by the PTL or Operations Officer on duty via the “OVA” reporting system as per HMC policy and complete a summary report, check list for radio system shut down. 4. Testing: 4.1 If there have been no failures of the system, the failover (backup) system will be tested and documented bi - annually .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 146 SOP 3.12 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.12 NJM SHUTDOWN Implementation Date 13 March 2018 Purpose To detail the backup systems available in managing calls for service and resources in the event where NJM is not available. Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS S TAFF 1. GENERAL GUIDELINES: 1.1 Staff are responsible for maintaining familiarization with these resources in the event of an unplanned NJM crash or scheduled system down time. 1.2 Ambulance Service Medical Dispatchers must routinely review NJM backup procedures and resources. 1.3 Each employee is responsible for soliciting assistance from the case controller or team leader if additional directions are needed. 1.4 Each employee must ensure they are adequately prepared to follow backup procedures should they become necessary. 2. SHUTDOWN PROCEDURE: 2.1 In the event of a NJM failure or scheduled maintenance: 2.1.1 The Operation Officer for Communications or the Distribution Team Leader should notify immediately the NJM helpd esk line 44526520 or 44526751 . 2.1.2 The Operati

ons Officer for Communications or the Distribution Team Leader must immediately notify the On - Call Communications Operations Manager (Silver Communications) a nd the On - Call Managers/Executive as per the notificatio n procedures. 2.1.3 The manual communications record ( Appendix 1 ) must be utilized to process calls for service and make dispatch assignments. Manual communications record sheets are located at each call - taking/dispatching console and on the Production/Distribution Team Leader desks. 2.1.4 A case controller will be assigned to coordinate and endorse the manual sheets between the call - takers and Dispatchers. 2.1.5 Manual entry will require the same information as NJM screen to be handwritten in the appropriate space on the manual communications record. 2.1.6 Before forwarding the sheet to be dispatcher, Call - taker will enter the necessary information (address, received time, priority of the call, call - taker name, and console number). The dispatch code will be forwarded when available. 2.1.7 Dispatcher is required to enter accurately all remaining information including time stamps. 2.1.8 The CFS opened during the shutdown will be numbered as follows: 2.1.8.1
1st CFS will take the number YY - MM - DD - 9001 ( Example: The 1 st case opened during the shutdown will be: 170521 9001 ).

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 147 2.1.8.2 2nd CFS will take the number YY - MM - DD - 9002 etc... Then the numbers will keep adding for each new CFS (opened during the shutdown) until NJM is back to operation. 2.1.8.3 These numbers during the shutdown will be coo rdinated by the on - duty DTL and will be written on the CFS - manual - sheet before providing it to the dispatcher. 2.1.8.4 The dispatcher will then verbalize the Shutdown’s CFS number to the Field Crew(s) via radio. 2.1.9 The running CFSs during the shutdown will maintain their original NJM CFS number and NO Shutdown’s CFS Number will be created for them. 2.1.10 The last 4 digits of the Shutdown’s CFS Number shall not be used twice during the same day. 2.1.11 If a shutdown happens twice during the same day the DTL will continue numbering the CFS starting from the last Shutdown’s CFS Number of the previous shutdown. 2.1.12 (Example: Two shutdowns happened in May 19th, 2017): 2.1.13 1st shutdown happened at 08:00 with 5 cases opened, the numbers will be as follows: 1705199001, 1705199002, 17051 99003, 1705199004 and 1705199005. 2.1.14 2nd shutdown happened at 14:30 with 2 cases opened the num

bers will be as follows: 1705199006, 1705199007. 2.1.15 If a shutdown happens the NEXT day the DTL will start numbering the CFS starting from 9001. 2.1.16 (Example: 1 shutdown ha ppened May 10th and another happened 11th): 2.1.17 1st shutdown happened May 12th at 22:00 with 3 cases opened, the numbers will be as follows: 1705109001, 1705109002 and 1705109003. 2.1.18 2nd shutdown happened May 11th at 10:30 with 4 cases opened the numbers will be as follows: 1705119001, 1705119002, 1705119003, 1705119004. 2.2 Once NJM has been restored: 2.2.1 Calls received during the shutdown will be logged into NJM immediately when NJM application is back in service. 2.2.2 A call - taker shall be assigned or a DTL to ensure that all manually opened CFSs are created inside NJM and a scanned copy of the CFS - Manual - sheet shall be attached to the case in NJM respectively. 2.2.3 Each CFS - Manual - sheet will contain the Shutdown’s CFS Numbe r and the “REAL” CFS number created when NJM is back to operation. 2.2.4 A Summary Checklist ( Appendix 2 ) with a clear crosscheck and link between “REAL” CFS number & “SHUTDOWN’S” CFS number will be sent by the DTL to the below ema ils: 2.2.4.1 nccquality@hama
d.qa 2.2.4.2 AS - NCCTeamLeadersMailGroup@hamad.qa 2.2.4.3 AmbulanceData@hamad.qa 2.2.4.4 ambulanceCDO@hamad.qa

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 148 2.3 The original copy of the summary checklist and all Manual CFSs shall be sent to the Operations Manager for Communication s then to the Quality Improvement Unit for filling. 2.4 If NJM is back after Midnight, the same a bove steps will apply. 2.5 PTL will record the time of the shutdown and the return back to service in the PTL Daily Report . 3. Documentation: 3.1 Following HMC policy, it is obligatory for the Production Team Leader (PTL) or the Operations Officer present during the shift to document all incidents of equipment failure. This documentation process should be carried out through the “OVA” reporting system. Additionally, they are required to finalize a summary report and a complete checklist, specifically focusi ng on the procedures for the NJM shutdown. This approach ensures that all equipment malfunctions are systematically recorded and managed, promoting both operational efficiency and accountability within the team .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 151 SOP 3.13 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.13 ACCESS TO THE H EALTHCARE COORDINATI ON CENTER (HCC) Implementation Date 01 October 2016 Purpose To maintain an access - controlled and secure communications centre environment to ensure uninterrupted service delivery . Responsibility ALL COMMUNICATIONS C ENTER STAFF 1. The HCC control rooms (Call taking and dispatch rooms) is considered as designated restricted areas . 2. Do not allow visitors to use the phones, radios, or other HCC equipment. 3. Requests for information, quality reviews, reports and miscellaneous business - related discussions with HCC employees should be conducted either by email, memorandum, inter - office telephone, or incident report. 4. Access Control: 4.1 Access shall be limited to staff working inside the HCC and parties with appropriate clearance (i.e. approved Execut ives, managers, operational officers and IT Project Managers), and shall enter the control rooms through authorized entry points only. 4.2 The primary areas shall be accessed by digital card

only. 4.3 Never loan your physical keys, badges, key cards, pin codes, or other physical access control devices to other employees or visitors. 4.4 Keep all doors (including doors of the communications/data equipment rooms) closed and secured to prevent any unauthorized access. 4.5 Do not use emergency exit doors for access; never bloc k or prop open emergency exits. 4.6 Never enter the control room using authorized access to conduct personal business. 4.7 Field/Operations personnel shall not be permitted entry into the control room. 4.8 Patients and family members are prohibited from entering the control rooms at any time. 4.9 When Patients and family members want to endorse the medical certificate should not enter the call center. 4.10 HCC personnel shall not hang around in the control room before or after their shift and shall not enter the control room during off - duty periods. 5. Visits to the HCC: 5.1 Visitation is a privilege allowed at the discretion of the management staff and may be revoked at any time. 5.2 Any visitor requiring admission to HCC shall contact either the Operations Officer (Oscar HCC), Operatio ns Manager/AED of HCC for permission. 5.3 In - room tours mus

t be pre - scheduled, and a Senior Supervisor shall be assigned to conduct any approved tours. 5.4 All visitors, vendors and non - direct employees shall be met and escorted (by HCC personnel) throughout the d uration of the visit and shall sign in and out in the log book before entering and upon leaving the premises .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 152 SOP 3.14 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.14 CALL - FOR - SERVIC E COORDINATION BETWE EN NATIONAL COMMAND CENTER A ND HEALTHCARE COORDI NATION CENTER Implementation Date 01 June 20 16 Purpose To maintain an access - controlled and secure communications center environment to ensure uninterrupted service d elivery . Responsibility ALL HMCAS HEALTHCARE COORDINATION SERVIC ES PERSONNEL 1. Only “legitimate” emergency shall be handled on the NCC 999 and hotlines systems . 2. Do not tie up the medical emergency telephone network with non - emergency calls since it has a limited number of dedicated trunks/lines/consoles. This could result in a “real” emergency call being delayed, blocked, or routed back to 999 operators. 3. Non - emergency and Routine Inter - facility calls received at NCC : 3.1 Patient’s Transport (non - emergency) calls received on emergency lines (999/hotline) at NCC . 3.1.1 All calls received through the hotline/999 will be treated as emergency calls and processed accordingly, unless: 3.1.1.1 The caller indicates

that the patient requires non - emergency transporta tion to a residence or a facility other than a hospital Emergency Department and/or , 3.1.1.2 The patient complaint is non - emergency and fits under the Patient Transport Services (PTS) criteria . 3.1.2 If a PTS call is received on the hotline/999 the call taker shall advi se the caller that they have called on an emergency line and shall direct the caller to dial the non - emergency line “16099” or “4439 8555”. Do not transfer the call to the HCC lines , since this may tie up the 999 and hotlines trunks. The following cases ar e exceptions and shall be treated as follows: 3.1.2.1 Doha International Airport ( DIA ) cases received at NCC : 3.1.2.1.1 Patients to be transported from DIA arrivals shall be transported in most of the cases to the emergency department and shall be considered as emergency cases, thus If patient transport request from DIA is received at NCC, and the following steps shall be t aken : 3.1.2.1.1.1 The call taker shall immediately process it in NJM using the most appropriate ProQA chief complaint. 3.1.2.1.1.2 The dispatcher shall ensure the closest alpha unit is dispatched first along with the closest Delta

unit (Delta8 or Delta7). A Charlie unit may be ad ded if required according to caller information and dispatch matrix. 3.1.2.1.1.3 Ideally dispatch units to DIA from Hub1 (standby at the Museum spoke, unless another alpha unit is closer) .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 153 3.1.2.1.1.4 The call taker and the dispatcher shall immediately notify the DTL about the ai rport case. 3.1.2.1.1.5 If call taker discovers the call to be case of a non - emergency transportation, he/she shall continue processing the call as an emergency and immediately notify the DTL. 3.1.2.1.1.6 The DTL shall then call back the caller to verify the nature of the call an d coordinate with the HCC to send appropriate resources prior to cancellation of the call at NCC. 3.1.2.1.1.7 In case NCC receives a case from Hamad International Airport (HIA) and the aircraft has landed/going to land in a short time . 3.1.2.1.1.8 The call shall be processed as a bove and NCC DTL should coordinate further with the HCC UTL to verify if there is an existing booking or an ambulance dispatched . 3.1.2.1.1.9 If there no booking or pre - notification given to NCC, NCC shall continue with the call or the closet or most appropriate ambul ance deployed from NCC or HCC . 3.2 HCC Interfacility calls received on emergency lines at NCC consist of all patients who have been seen by a nurse or doctor and need further evaluation (higher level of ca

re) or transfer (to a more appropriate facility) from Hamad General Hospital, Heart Hospital, Women’s Hospital, Women’s Wellness and Research Center (WWRC), Al Khor Hospital, Al Wakra Hospital, Cuban Hospital, Pediatric Emergency Centers (PEC) Al Saad and Al Rayyan, Sidra Medicine, Hazm M e ba i re ek General Hospital and Primary Health Care Corporation (PHCC) facilities. 3.2.1 Interfacility Protocol 45/46/47 Cases ( Appendix A ) : All calls coming from hospitals listed in Appendix A , Sidra or Primary Health Care Corpo ration (PHCC) facilities should be processed at HCC and ProQA shall be completed under most appropriate protocol. On the occasion that these cases come to NCC: 3.2.1.1 Verify the exact location of the patient to ensure the transfer request is being initiated from WITHIN the facility (in a bed) . 3.2.1.2 If the patient is OUTSIDE of the facility , (means patient not under care of the facility or not registered/admitted) NCC will proceed with the call using Protocols 1 - 32 (in case HCC received such cases the call taker should direct the caller to call 999 (except the PHCC cases ). 3.2.1.3 If the patient is INSIDE of the facility, NCC will proceed with the call using

Protocol 37 “Transfer/Interfacility/Palliative Care” and Protocol 33 exclusively for PHCC calls . 3.2.1.3.1 The call taker should immediately notify the NCC DTL who will contact the HCC UTL to confirm if there is any technical issue with the Cisco Phone system or busyness/availability of the call takers as well as to confirm the availability of the SAS resources, However NCC shall continue with the call (using NJM ) if no HCC unit available/more closer or appropriate and if

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 154 there is available unit the HCC shall advise the unit to switch the appropriate NCC channel . 3.2.1.3.1.1 If there is no technical issue, an OVA can be raised against the caller. 3.2.1.4 The Oscar NCC will be consulted and decide if the responding NCC units should continue, or if the case should be reassigned to HCC. 3.3 Interfacility Protocol 33 Cases ( Appendix A ) : All calls coming from medical facilities otherwise identified as Protocol 33 facilities shall be processed at NCC and ProQA shall be completed under chief complaint 33 “Transfer/Inter - facility/Palliative Care . ” 4. NCC requiring special unit(s) from HCC (e.g. Bariatric, wheelchair, ICU) : 4.1 To manage emergency calls, the NCC team may need to utilize units from the Scheduled Ambulance Service (SAS). The use of those units sh all be shortened to the duration of the relevant emergency call and shall return immediately to the pool of the SAS units once cleared available. Take the following steps into consideration any time a specific SAS unit is need at NCC: 4.1.1 The NCC DTL shall inform the HCC UTL that a special unit is required. 4.1.1.1 The NCC DTL

may utilize the HCC CAD Tactical Board to view the location and status of available units to assist in choosing the closest and most appropriate unit based on the unit(s) type required, not the unit(s) name. 4.1.2 The HCC UTL shall check the availability of the requested unit(s) and decide which unit to give NCC. 4.1.3 If the unit(s) is not immediately available, the HCC UTL will update the NCC DTL once the special unit is available. The NCC DTL shal l follow up with the HCC and/or seek an alternative. 4.1.4 If the unit(s) is available, the HCC UTL shall instruct the HCC Dispatcher to inform the unit to change to the required NCC operational channel and update the NCC DTL accordingly. 4.1.4.1 HCC Dispatcher will pla ce the Call Sign of the selected SAS resource in VLT CAD ‘ UNAVAILABLE’ . 4.1.5 The NCC DTL shall inform the PTL to publish the special unit in NJM so it can be assigned by the dispatcher in the relevant Call for Service (CFS). 4.1.6 NCC Dispatcher monitors the status o f the Call Sign throughout the incident response through to CLEAR , 4.1.6.1 The unit shall be instructed to go back the HCC operational channel immediately after finishing the NCC case . 4.1.6.2 NCC Dis

patcher will advise the NCC DTL when the Call Sign is CLEAR, 4.1.6.3 NCC DTL not ifies HCC UTL that the Call Sign is CLEAR . 5. HCC requiring special unit(s) from NCC (e.g. Alpha, Charlie, Delta, LifeFlight) : 5.1 The use of Emergency Service (ES) units shall be shortened to the duration of the relevant IFT call and shall return immediately to the pool of the ES units once cleared available. The below steps shall be taken into consideration any time a specific AS unit is needed by HCC: 5.1.1 HCC UTL shall inform the NCC DTL that EAS resource is required to respond to SAS case.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 155 5.1.2 Charlie r equests are exempted which should be coordinated directly with NCC DTL through Retrieval Desk - EMD as needed, However the RD - EMD must update the UTL and timely escalate if there is challenges to obtain CCP from the NCC DTL . 5.1.2.1 Details include the priority, inc ident location of the case and designated radio channel. 5.1.3 NCC DTL coordinates resource assignment with NCC Dispatcher and NCC DTL advises HCC UTL the Call Sign they will receive. 5.1.3.1 NCC Dispatcher will inform the unit to change to the required HCC radio channel and advice the case response priority (P1 or P2) and shall place the Call Sign of the selected EAS resource in NJM CAD as ‘ UNAVAILABLE w/HCC .’ 5.1.4 HCC UTL notifies the HCC Dispatcher of the Call Sign they will receive. 5.1.5 HCC Dispatcher dispatches the Call Sign in VTL CAD and calls the Call Sign on the radio. 5.1.6 Call Sign comes up on the designated radio channel acknowledging the priority, incident location, and responding from location. 5.1.7 HCC Dispatcher advan ces the status of the Call Sign in VTL CAD and provides any additional details, confirming any det

ails that may have been missed, 5.1.7.1 If HCC Dispatch unable to reach the Call Sign within 2 minutes, HCC UTL will recontact the NCC DTL. 5.1.8 HCC Dispatcher monitors th e status of the Call Sign throughout the incident response through to CLEAR, 5.1.8.1 The unit shall be instructed to go back the NCC operational channel immediately after finishing the HCC case . 5.1.8.2 HCC Dispatcher will advise the HCC UTL when the Call Sign is CLEAR, 5.1.8.3 H CC UTL notifies NCC DTL that the Call Sign is CLEAR. 6. Medical/Trauma Emergencies calls received at HCC : 6.1 Medical/Trauma emergency calls : 6.1.1 If a call of an emergency nature is received at HCC non - emergency lines, the HCC call taker shall advise the caller to ca ll back on 999. Do not transfer the call to the NCC hotline .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 156 SOP 3.15 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Communications Procedure 3.15 CAPTURING UNIT STATUS IN MDT Implementation Date 01 June 2016 Purpose • To establish procedures for the use of the Mobile Data Terminals (MDT's) and to define dispatcher and field unit responsibilities . • To define the differentiation between the unit status definition with examples . Responsibility ALL OPERATIONS, COMM UNICATION STAFF & CL INICAL FIELD RESPONDERS 1. In line with the technical enhancement in HMCAS most of the response units are equipped with Mobile Data Terminals (MDT) and Automated Vehicle Locator (AVL). 2. GENERAL GUIDELINES : 2.1 Provides direct access to the Ministry of Interior Computer - Aided Dispatch system called “Najem” (NJM) which is used by HMC Ambulance Service. Starting from March 9th, 2015, all equipped units shall use MDTs and AVL as the primary tool for rece iving and acknowledging routine dispatch assignments and updating unit status, with due regard to crew safety. 2.2 MDTs shall: 2.2.1 not be installed, removed, moved, or modified in any manner

by anyone other than authorized Department or technical service personnel . 2.2.2 not be activated or used by anyone other than those personnel properly trained in their use. At no time shall an employee use another log - on I.D. number or password other than their own. 2.2.3 used to transmit official messages only 2.2.4 not be used to transmit any message of a defamatory, derogatory, inflammatory, racist, or sexual nature. 2.3 All MDT transmissions shall be recorded (in some cases, may become public information) and are subject to audit and periodic review for appropriateness and conformance, by the Au diting and Review section. There will be no expectations of confidentiality or privacy. 2.4 All transmissions shall be subject to constant monitoring by Supervisory and Operations personnel. Personnel found to be making personal or inappropriate transmissions using MDTs or radios are subject to disciplinary action. Examples of prohibited transmissions, but not limited to, include: 2.4.1 Unnecessary radio conversations or MDT transmissions; 2.4.2 Argumentative or sarcastic transmissions on the radio or MDT; 2.4.3 Us e of profane and obscene language; 2.4.4 Use of personal names durin

g transmission, except in an emergency; 2.5 No member of HMCAS shall access, disclose, nor knowingly permit the access to or disclosure of information within the possession of HMCAS to any person or entity except as necessary in the performance of his/her official duties and in compliance with departmental policy. 2.6 MDT’s assigned to employees may be used in training projects/courses and or drills, when the training or drill is related to course work approved by HMCAS. Relevant staff shall receive Communication manager approval prior to using MDT’s for training/drills.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 157 2.7 Employees shall not use MDT’s, whether on or off duty, for the tracking of any outside employment, personal business, or profit - making ventures. All records and information maintained in any MDT assigned to any employee are confidential, the property of HMCAS, and must be made available for inspection to any department supervisor or manager. 2.8 Patient history information, personnel informat ion or history, intelligence information, or other confidential information may not be maintained in MDT’s unless authorized by an operations manager or higher authority, as part of an ongoing investigation. 2.9 Any field unit in the “AVAILABLE on the move” or “Available on STANDBY” status is presumed to be inside the unit and monitoring the MDT for assignments and messages, when away from the unit and not monitoring the MDT, field personnel shall inform the Dispatcher via radio. On the other hand, any status c hange or acknowledgment that is broadcast by radio voice by a field unit shall be entered in NJM by the dispatcher to avoid errors and duplication of effort. 2.10 Field personnel signed onto MDT’s shall be responsibl

e for monitoring the terminal and maintaining correct unit status on the CAD system. Any field unit in the “AVAILABLE” or “STANDBY” status is presumed to be inside the unit and monitoring the MDT for assignments and messages. 2.11 MDT’s shall be used as the default dispatch tool and whenever practical to reduce radio traffic. Obvious crew safety or urgent information will continue to be broadcast via voice. The field unit has the ultimate decision over when they will transition to voice as situations in the field change. 3. PROCEDURE : 3.1 Field crew assigned to r esponse units equipped with Mobile Data Terminals shall consider the following: 3.2 MDT connectivity continuously , dispatchers shall monitor MDTs connectivity via the CAD system (NJM) by verifying continuously the MDT signals (RED/ORANGE/GREEN) in the NJM disp atch interface. In case of disconnection, the dispatcher shall follow up with the unit via the radio to re - establish connection of MDTs with the control room. The units may try the following: 3.2.1 Change location for better signal 3.2.2 “log off” the MDT then “log on ” again 3.3 Log - on : Field Crew shall log on the MDT at the beginning of the shift and shall t

est the connectivity with the control room. 3.4 Initial Dispatch : 3.4.1 Receive calls for service via MDT; 3.4.2 Dispatch shall utilize a "minimum voice" technique. Voice dispatch onl y the heading and location of the incident, (i.e. “Alpha One One, Zulu case in Madinat Khalifa south”). 3.4.3 Acknowledge receipt of calls for service by transmitting appropriate MDT “unit status” and by voice through the TETRA Radio. 3.4.4 MDT’s may not receive updat ed information automatically; field crew shall press the “REFRESH" key to receive updates. 3.4.5 Dispatch will voice broadcast any critical supplements and changes to the call while the units are responding. 3.5 Unit Status : (refer to Figure 3.15 - 1 “NJM - Unit Status Definition Document”): 3.5.1 “Unit status” shall be captured in NJM to determine the current operational status/availability of response Units as well as recording when they are responding or

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 158 proceeding to incidents. The MDT allows Field crews to directly change their own status within NJM. The filed crew shall use the relevant “unit status” as per their movement and position. 3.5.2 Field crew shall change their status and provide updates via MDT. The response unit may change to radio voice as situations in the field change, to such an extent that it is not physically possible for the crew to update the MDT. In these situations, any unit status broadcasted through the radio should be entered in NJM by the dispatcher to avoid errors and duplication of efforts. 3.5.3 At the end of the case, responders shall assign their unit “CLEARED” from the case via the MDT. The dispatcher shall then assign them “ASSIGNED AVAILABLE”; the unit shall acknowledge “ASSIGNED AVAILABLE” via the radio. 3.5.4 If the unit is cleared from the call but not available for service for any reason (mechanical/other), the crew shall inform the Dispatcher who shall advise them to change to the production radio channel and coordinate with the Production Team leader (PTL). If required, the PTL may assign them “U navailable status”. The PTL s

hall monitor the time and validity of all units assigned “’Unavailable status”. 3.5.5 To avoid confusion regarding who/when/how to update the responding unit’s status, the following guidelines have been created. Kindly take note of t he important rules, definitions, and scenarios below: 3.6 The Main Rules : 3.6.1 It is the responsibility of the responding crew to update their unit’s status timely via MDT (or via TETRA radio when MDT is not working). 3.6.2 If the MDT freezes/hangs up while enroute to a call, the crew shall immediately notify the dispatcher via TETRA radio. The dispatcher shall thereafter take over the MDT status responsibility from the crew until the unit becomes active again. 3.6.3 The dispatcher shall document the reason in the comments box as to why he/she performed the update (examples: “MDT is hanging. Responding crew cannot update.” “The unit failed to update the status even after being advised to do so. Unit is already on scene.”) 3.6.4 The responding crew shall send their unit’s status updat e via TETRA radio when: 3.6.4.1 a call is received . The crew shall update their status as they begin to move toward the scene. 3.6.4.2 with the patient (the dispatcher

will update this status) . 3.6.4.3 “clear”/“available” . The crew will update their status as “clear” and the disp atcher will update the crew status as “available.” 3.6.5 If two unit’s status are transmitted simultaneously, they shall be both entered in the order described in Figure13.5B: 3.6.5.1 Example1: If the crew arrives at the scene and the patient at the same time; they shal l enter “on scene” then “with patient” consequently with no time delay. 3.6.5.2 Example2: if the crew are already on the move to a standby location and they receive a call via MDT/RADIO they shall enter “Received” and “to scene” consequently with no time delay.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 159 3.6.6 ‘ ’Available’’ status : 3.6.6.1 Available - In position : Unit is available to be dispatched from its assigned standby location/position. It is staffed correctly, has passed the Key Vehicle Safety Check and has complete Equipment and Communication Technology Minimum Equipment . 3.6.6.2 Available - En route to position : Unit is available to be dispatched but is repositioning/enroute to its assigned location/position. It is staffed correctly, has passed the Key Vehicle Safety Check and has complete Equipment and Communi cation Technology Minimum Equipment. 3.6.6.3 Available Admin : Unit is available to be dispatched but the crew is engaged in management approved admin activities. It is staffed correctly, has passed the Key Vehicle Safety Check and has complete Equipment and Communication Technology Minimum Equipment . 3.6.7 ‘’Unavailable status : 3.6.7.1 Unavailable Mechanical issue : Unit is unavailable due to a mechanical/vehicle issue/RTA but it is staffed correctly and has complete Equipment and Communication Technology Minimum Equipment . 3.6.7.2 Unavailable Equipment issue : Unit is unavailable due to an Eq

uipment or Communication Technology issue but is staffed correctly and has passed the Key Vehicle Safety Check. 3.6.7.3 Unavailable Crew issue : Unit is unavailable due to a crew issue/incomplete crew b ut it has passed the Key Vehicle Safety Check and has complete Equipment and Communication Technology Minimum Equipment. 3.6.7.4 Unavailable Refueling : Unit is unavailable as it requires to or is refueling. It is staffed correctly, has passed the Key Vehicle Safet y Check and has complete Equipment and Communication Technology Minimum Equipment. 3.6.7.5 Unavailable with NCC/HCC : Unit is not available to be dispatched as it is currently reassigned to the NCC/ HCC. 3.6.8 LF status: 3.6.8.1 Any LF related status (Standby - Activate - Startup - On station - Landed Hospital) must be updated by lF coordinated accordingly and timely . 3.6.9 “With the Patient” versus “On Scene”: 3.6.9.1 “With the Patient” Status : This refers to a situation where the crew is on the location of the incident and is standing next to t he patient. The crew shall immediately update their status to the NCC via TETRA radio, by transmitting “with the patient .” 3.6.9.1.1 On Scene” Status Scenarios . Cases Yes No

Comments 1. You have the exact/actual address of the incident, for instance "QARS"(Qatar Area Referencing System) or street number with gate number. Is the unit 10/10? þ ---- 2. You have the meeting/rendezvous point (a roundabout or a landmark). 3. Is the unit 10/10? þ ----

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 160 4. The exact location of the incident is unknown due to language barrier or the caller is new in Qatar and only general area is given. For example, the caller location is in Al Rayan Qadeem (Old Rayan). The unit enters this area. Is the unit 10/10? þ The 10/10 is the CFS location sign. Otherwise, the 10/10 is the general or main area. 3.6.9.1.2 Confusing Scenarios . Cases Yes No Comments 1. In a case of RTA (Road Traffic Accident) on a highway and the unit happens to be on the other side of the road where the accident is and needs to take the next bridge or R/A to reach the scene, is the unit 10/10? þ The unit shall inform NCC that they can see the RTA, but they will take U - turn on the bridge or roundabout before they reach the scene. Dispatcher shall document this in the comment box. 2. The initial location given is reached by the unit and then the caller suddenly changes the location and gave a new one. Is the unit 10/10? þ The unit is 10/10 in the first location. The dispatcher shall document the changes in location and the secondary 10/10 in th e comments box. 3. The unit is following the caller but the CFS loca

tion is still very far. Is the unit 10/10? þ ---- 4. A unit is on the same street as in CFS location and the unit can see the caller but cannot reach the location due to road obstructions (examples: flood, heavy traffic, RTA with no victim, road closure, etc.). Is the unit 10/10? þ The unit shall inform the dispatcher that they can actually see the caller, but they cannot proceed to the location due to some road barriers. Di spatcher will document this in the comment(s) box. 5. The unit reported “following the Civil Defence in a case of fire” or “following the police in a case of assault or RTA”. Is the unit 10/10? þ The answer is yes if the police or Civil Defence guides the crew to the location which is known to them. 6. A call is inside a building (residential/commercial, high - security facility like HIA, Emiri Guard, Doha Port, QP Industrial) but the responding unit is already outside its gate. Is the unit 10/10? þ ---- 3.7 Cancellations and Exchanges : 3.7.1 Cancellation of units by dispatch : Any cancellation of unit response to a call by dispatch shall be broadcast by voice. Field units shall acknowledge by voice. Dispatch shall be responsible for immediate CAD updates. 3.7

.2 Re - directing a unit from a currently assigned call : Dispatch shall voice broadcast the cancellation of the first call before assigning them to the new call. Exchanges will be done by voice and dispatch shall be responsible for the immediate NJM and CAD updates. 3.8 Changing Unit Location/Transport/Status : 3.8.1 Field units shall use the MDT to change locations whenever practical. If the unit chooses to voice the update, the dispatcher shall change the unit status in CAD. Terminal to terminal messages shall not be used to change location or status. 3.9 Dispatch Script/ProQA data (information a bout the scene and the patient):

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 161 3.9.1 Dispatch Script information is automatically sent to the MDT. It will be the field unit's responsibility to read the premise history (ProQA scripted questions/answer). 3.9.2 The dispatcher shall give the dispatch script by voice to units not equipped with MDT or any time an MDT is not operational. This is applicable for initial dispatch and for backup units. 3.9.3 The dispatch script shall contain information about the incident and the patient as per ProQA scripted questions and a s per the answers given by the caller. The dispatch script may contain information such as patient’s chief complaint, Mechanism of injury, number of patients, patient’s age, and other information (exact location of the patient flat/floor). 3.9.4 The dispatch scr ipt shall be displayed in the MDT in English. If the MDT displays in Arabic language, the field crew shall immediately notify the control room. 3.10 Emergency Messages : 3.10.1 If the HMCAS Control room receives an MDT request for emergency assistance, the procedure wi ll be as follows: 3.10.1.1 If the unit is on a call, dispatch shall immediately send a backup unit and then at

tempt to raise the unit on the relevant radio channel to confirm status. Dispatcher will broadcast by voice that “help is on their way.” 3.10.1.2 If the unit is not currently on a call, dispatch will immediately attempt to raise the unit on the relevant channel and confirm location and status. 3.10.1.3 As soon as practical, the unit declaring the emergency shall contact dispatch via voice to confirm the nature of the situatio n. 3.10.1.4 The MDT does not have to be logged on to send an emergency message. 3.11 Message Acknowledgment : 3.11.1 Do not assume that messages or information sent via the MDT to dispatch or other MDT’s have been read or that action has been taken without receiving acknowledgm ent. 3.11.1.1 Log off : It is the field unit’s responsibility to properly log off the MDT at the end of the shift by using the LOGOFF command. The MDT shall not be logged off during the shift unless there is operation failure. The MDT/MDC should not be powered off w hile it is still logged on. This causes the system to continuously attempt to reach the terminal and can dramatically slow responses to other system users. 3.11.1.2 Security : The MDT may be disabled for security reasons by

using the "LOCK" function as needed whenev er the user is away from the unit. To reactivate the MDT, use the "UNLOCK" function. Do not use the camera of the MDT for any reason. 3.11.1.3 MDT Operation Failure : If an MDT fails to operate properly the crew assigned to the unit shall perform trouble shooting pr ocedures before declaring failure. If the MDT is still not working, the crew shall notify dispatch immediately via voice that the unit no longer has MDT capability. It will be the MDT user responsibility to fill out an "MDT failure" form and follow the pro cedure of changing the MDT. 4. CARE, CLEANING, AND SERVICE OF MOBILE DATA TERMINALS :

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 162 4.1 All requests for installation, removal, or modification shall be forwarded in writing to the Communications technical support (or communication Production supervisor). 4.2 No equipment or items may be attached to MDT other than those specifically designed to be a part of the MDT by the manufacturer. 4.3 MDT display screens shall be cleaned with commercial glass cleaner and a soft cloth only. 4.4 MDT keyboards shall be c leaned using alcohol and a soft cloth. DO NOT use abrasives, solvents, or acetone to clean MDTs. 4.5 Spillage of any type must be removed from the MDT surface as quickly as possible using a soft clean cloth. Any incident of spillage shall be reported on the "M DT Service" form and forwarded to the Communications technical support (Communications production Supervisor). 4.6 Required repair or service of MDT’s shall be properly documented on the designated MDT repair form. Completed forms shall be turned in to service personnel as quickly as possible after the failure is noted. 5. Automated Vehicle Locator (AVL): 5.1 As part of the technical solution to ensure effective and safe utilization of Ambula

nce Service resources all units are installed with an AVL system. 5.2 Dispatchers shall use the AVL tracking system to assist in assigning the appropriate unit to a call. 5.3 Dispatchers shall use the AVL tracking system to guide units more accurately towards the scene of an incident, should the MDT fail. 5.4 The AVL does not replac e MDT or radio procedures. 5.5 The AVL system allows the control room to pinpoint the exact location of all units at all times, ensuring the safety of responding crews by facilitating a quick response for any type of assistance needed. 6. Responsibility NATIONAL COMMAND CEN TRE 1. The National Command Centre (NCC) shall host a bank of phones that will be used for on call purposes. 2. The primary use of these phones is so that HMCAS staff can reach a particular call sign at the same number always, regardless of the actual individual/person responsible for the duties and responsibilities associated with that call sign. 3. The Major Incident Response (MIR) On Call Roster shall be sent out from the HMCAS Emergency Services office each Sunday. 4. The Oscar NCC shall: 4.1 Review the worksheet, locating those MIR call signs that have a dedicated On - Call phone. 4.2 Forward the dedicated phone line to the associated HMCAS staff members specified contact number. 4.3 Do a test call to the MIR dedicated phone number to ensure that the HMCAS s

taff member who is supposed to receive the call, actually receives the call. 5. During the routine afternoon MIR radio communications check, the NCC shall do a test call to the MIR call signs with a dedicated - On Call phone.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 166 SOP 3.17 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Operations and Production Procedure 3.17 MANAGEMENT OF C ALL SIGN COMMUNICATI ONS EQUIPMENT SETS (CCES) Implementation Date 01 June 2016 Purpose To identify the activities and processes to be used in distribution and management of call sign communications equipment sets for all response units . Responsibility PRODUCTION MANAGERS AND SUPERVISORS, TEA M LEADERS, AND HMCAS OPERATIONAL ST AFF 1. Definitions : 1.1 Call sign communications equipment sets (CCES) – a Protection Case with a set of communications equipment required for a vehicle to be activated as a call sign. 1.2 Vehicle – an ambulance, a response car, or a bus, etc. with a registration number . 1.3 Unit – a vehicle with a call sign 2. General Guidelines : 2.1 HMCAS operational staff shall : 2.1.1 Ensure familiarization with the use of CCES 2.1.2 Be responsible for CCES while registered to them in line with this SOP . 2.1.3 Request assistance if additional training or direction is needed for the use of CCES. 3. Use of Protection Case and its Contents : 3

.1 The Protection Case is principally intended as a means of protection for communication equipment/devices (ePCR, NJM/MDT pad, TETRA radios, mobile phone, battery chargers, etc.) 3.2 Staff shall use the Protection Case only for storing the above - mentioned device s. 4. Actions by Emergency/Non - Emergency Operational Staff at Commencement of Shift : 4.1 Collect the CCES from either the unit’s previous crew or from the Hub Production Supervisor if the unit is coming onto shift. 4.2 Note the serial number of the CCES and write it on the vehicle checklist . 4.3 Check the battery level of the devices and recharge if required. 4.4 As soon as practically possible, complete a vehicle checklist which will include the CCES. 4.5 Ensure that the serial numbers, physical presence, and serviceability of a ll CCES items are recorded and reported to Communications immediately after checks . If the unit is required to attend a call before completing the check, comply at the earliest practicable time, normally on completion of the call. 4.6 If a unit’s vehicle needs to be changed, the attendant shall transfer the CCES to the new vehicle . The Hub Production supervisor shall advise the PTL accordi

ngly. 4.7 CCES items shall be kept in their charging dock, where provided, when the attendant is in the vehicle, especially when the vehicle is on the move. 4.8 Unless handing over directly to the incoming crew at the end of the shift, carefully replace the items to the Protection Case so that it is as you would expect to find it . Where applicable, both

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 167 crew members shall be responsibl e for the CCES pack and need to agree that the pack is complete. 4.9 If the Protection Case has some missing items: 4.9.1 Record this on the vehicle checklist 4.9.2 Follow procedure as per Section 5 5. Actions by Emergency and Non - Emergency Staff in the Event of Equipment Loss, Failure/Damage, and Theft : 5.1 Advise the PTL and Hub Production supervisor as appropriate and act upon their instructions. 5.2 If stolen or lost, the Hub Production supervisor shall notify PTL to inform the police within 48 hours. 5.3 Complete an inci dent report 5.4 Line management shall provide a replacement CCES and shall interview staff to establish the circumstances surrounding the loss, theft, or damage. 5.5 Hand over a faulty item to the Hub Production Supervisor for replacement and label as “Faulty Equi pment” on a RED tag. 5.6 Complete and sign an OVA and defect form after receipt of the new device/equipment. 5.7 Register the new device with the Hub production supervisor who shall notify the PTL. 5.8 The Hub Production Supervisor shall send the faulty unit back by to the Main Store for repair. 5.9 I

n the case of damage, loss, or a protection case locking fault, CCESs shall be swapped out. 6. Battery Charging of Equipment/Devices by staff : 6.1 Ensure that equipment/devices are charged at all times. 6.2 Outgoing crew shall endorse t he CCES to the incoming crew with equipment/devices in good/full battery level. 6.3 Never leave the devices out of charge 6.4 Charging stations are available in the spokes or hubs. Non - Emergency ePCR and MDT units can be charged in the vehicle docks. 6.5 If the batter y is not charging/faulty, refer to Section 5 7. Distribution of CCESs 7.1 Issuing CCES : 7.1.1 The initial issue of CCESs shall be coordinated centrally by the equipment Store. Production Managers in the Store shall ensure that CCESs have been delivered to the hubs and that they are signed for by the hub production supervisor. Hub Production Supervisor shall ensure that the Serial Number is recorded on the form at the time of signing. 7.1.2 Issuance of CCESs to the Training shall be managed by the Training Supervisor. 7.2 Reco rd Keeping/Documentation : 7.2.1 HMCAS shall maintain a database of equipment contained in CCESs via an asset tracking system. 7.2.2 Equipment shall be bar -
coded and scanned

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 168 7.2.3 Logistics shall have access to the database for monitoring and checking purposes. This procedur e introduces one equipment tag, one Equipment Exchange Record (see Figure 3.17 - 1 – Equipment Exchange Record) and station whiteboards. 7.3 Managing Buffer Stock : 7.3.1 The purpose of buffer stock is to ensure crews can have an immediate replacement in the event of lo ss or damage of the CCES. 7.3.2 Hub Production Supervisors shall maintain a stock of replacement equipment and ensure that arrangements are in place for accessing this stock for emergency replacements on a 24/7 basis. 7.4 Scheduled Maintenance/Checking : 7.4.1 Equipment/de vices shall be maintained and repaired by approved contractors in accordance with manufacturer’s recommendations and guidelines . This includes function tests and scheduled servicing where appropriate. This process shall be facilitated by the Support Servic es and shall be undertaken either at the Main Store or at the ambulance stations/hubs. 7.4.2 Production Supervisors shall ensure that equipment is made available for maintenance (if informed of pending service visits). 7.4.3 A dat

abase of all individual items of equip ment which are maintained according to the schedule of the contractor maintained by the equipment supplier. 7.4.4 Maintenance contractors provide details of all equipment serviced on their visits and this information shall be placed on the database and may be us ed by Logistics to identify any items missed. These items will then be recalled to the Logistics for servicing within the approved timetables. 7.5 Audit : 7.5.1 The PTL shall ensure that a complete level register of CCESs issued to hubs is maintained, updated, and sent to the Director of Health Care Coordination Services and the Director of Support Services on the last day of each year. 7.5.2 The Head of Training Section and Production Supervisor shall ensure the audit of all CCESs distributed to the Training section for education purposes and shall send a copy of the report to the Director of Health Care Coordination Services and the Director of Support S ervices on the last day of each year.
