Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 541 SOP 6.2 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Operations Procedure 6.2 ADVERSE WEATHER PREP AREDNESS, RESPONSE A ND RECOVERY Implementation Date 9 July 2019 Purpose Establish a coordinated response during adverse weather to ensure the health and safety of Qatar and its population . Responsibility ALL HMCAS STAFF AND DIRECTORATES 1. Purpose : 1.1 The intent of this procedure is to establish a coordinated response during severe weather such as dust and rain to ensure the health and safety of Qatar and its population. This SOP will ensure that all role players within the HMCAS will provide adequate r esources, manage the resources, and ensure that the community has sufficient medical coverage . 1.2 Establish internal proactive measures to minimize disruption to key AS services due to severe or adverse weather events based on the following objectives: (Align to ISO 31000:2009; 22301:2013 ) . 1.2.1 Reduce the likelihood of a disruption 1.2.2 Shorten the period of disruption 1.2.3 Limit the impact of a disruption 1.3 Establish and outline additional measures by th

e ambulance service to prepare for, respond to , and recover from severe or extreme weather events. These measures are intended be in conjunction with established procedures outlined in the HMCAS S OP manual, the Ambulance Service Operations Plan (AS - 9032), and the A mbulance Service Major Incident Response Guide . 2. Definitions : 2.1 Severe weather: Dangerous meteorological or hydro - meteorological phenomenon, of varying duration, with risk of causing major damage, serious social disruption, and loss of human life. E.g. Heavy Rainfall and Flooding, High Winds, Tornado, etc . 2.2 Extreme Weather: Unexpected, unusual, unpredictable, unseasonal weather; weather at the extremes of the historical distribution * (outside the range that has been seen in the past*) E.g. Heat Wave, Cold Wave, etc. 2.3 Lead Time: The t ime available for preparation between an announcement or decision and the event it pertains to . 3. Procedures : 3.1 Specific Measures and activities are outlined in this document are divided into three activity phases along with the associated event trigger, trigg ering events must be sufficient enough as to present a threat to life and / or hampering or disrupting regul

ar AS service delivery . 3.2 Preparedness : 3.2.1 Event and emergency planning : 3.2.1.1 Ensure the regular conduct and documentation of Hazard Vulnerability Assessment (H VA), Threat Hazard Identification and Risk Assessments (THIRA), and / or Business Impact Analysis (BIA) activities as part of the preparedness planning . 3.2.1.2 Arrange for additional resources in anticipation of the event. ( SOP 4.10.B ) .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 542 3.2.1.3 Prepare or update the severe / extreme weather operational plan . 3.2.1.4 Facilitate the preparation of business continuity plans from all HMCAS directorate s. Activity Phase: Trigger: I. Preparedness a. Anticipated seasonal climate and weather changes. (e.g. winter / summer) . b. Severe or Extreme weather warning or advisory received. (e.g. 12 - 72 hours, meteorological prediction, weather forecast, etc.) c. PRIOR warning events II. Response a. Daily operations or response operations ongoing sudden severe weather or extreme weather event encountered. b. No prior warning event (e.g. sudden rain and flooding, fog conditions, increasing heat and humidity etc.) c. NO warning events III. Recovery a. Weather conditions significantly improved; weather advisory signals normal weather conditions predicted. b. Operations units stood down, normalizing conditions encountered by crews. All Clear. 4. Notification Process : 4.1 The On - duty AS - LifeFlight coordinator assigned to NCC will regularly monitor the weather as part of his regularly assigned duties . 4.2 Prevailing weather conditions will be updated daily to Osca

r - NCC by the LifeFlight coordinato r/ . 4.3 NCC Oscar to assess the need to issue a warning or initiate escalation procedures for a potential weather event. The need for a warning or initiating escalation should be assessed on two major considerations : 4.3.1 The likelihood of an event 4.3.2 Proceed with planned escalation processes (Policy 9032 ) 4.4 Oscar NCC should notify Silver Command 4.5 Silver C ommand notifies Gold Command and a decision to be made to : 4.5.1 Notify On Call personnel 4.5.2 Proceed with planned escalation processes (Policy 9032)

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 543 4.6 Once a decision has been made to issue a warning, Oscar NCC will notify via SMS/ fact 24 all relevant ambulance service directorates. SMS notifications will have a prefix followed by the advisory statement. A statement is either issued as a follow - up message to a warning, watch, or emergency, that may update, extend, or cancel the message i t is following up or a notification of significant weather for which no type of advisory, watch, or warning exists . Ex. SMS message: (Prefix)_ Emergency : (Statement)_” Flooding/ dust along xxxx areas, with expected further rain for the next xxxx hours, staf f advised......” 4.7 These statements are used in conjunction with specific weather phenomena to convey different levels of risk. In order of increasing risk, these statements are: 4.7.1 Outloo k: A Hazardous Weather Outlook is issued daily to indicate that a hazardo us weather or hydrologic event may occur in the next several days. The outlook will include information about potential severe thunderstorms, heavy rain or flooding, winter weather, extremes of heat or cold, etc., that may develop over the next seven days wi

th an emphasis on the first 24 hours of the forecast . 4.7.2 Advisor y: An advisory is issued when a hazardous weather or hydrologic event is occurring, imminent, or likely. Advisories are for "less serious" conditions than warnings that may cause significant in convenience, and if caution is not exercised could lead to situations that may threaten life or property . 4.7.3 Emergency: An Emergency is issued when an event that by itself cannot pose a threat to life or property but may indirectly cause other events to happe n that may pose a threat to life or property . 4.7.4 Watch: A watch is used when the risk of a hazardous weather or hydrologic event has increased significantly, but its occurrence, location, or timing is still uncertain. It is intended to provide enough lead tim e so those who need to set their plans in motion can do so. A watch means that hazardous weather is possible . 4.7.5 Warning: A warning is issued when a hazardous weather or hydrologic event is occurring, imminent, or likely. A warning statement issued means weat her conditions pose a threat to life or property . 5. Silver Command Actions: 5.1 Coordinate with NCC/NHICC to stop all hospital IFTs to allow mo

re resources to service the community in the event of disaster from the adverse weather . 5.2 Coordinate with NHICC to check with ED HGH Nursing Director if still there is Bed Crisis at ED . 5.3 Arrange for Extra Stretcher from HMC AS to be used for patient at ED if needed . 5.4 Coordinate with HMCAS Gold On call the Possibility of Divert and Wait for Confirmation . 5.5 Activate Silver Production to provide Extra Supply and consumable at ED HGH . 5.6 Monitor the turnaround time for the units to provide more resources to service the call demand . 6. Silv er Production Actions : 6.1 On call Team will coordinate with support services, VCS and schedule to arrange extra staffing, Vehicles and stock . 6.2 Extra units will be deployed as per the demand where areas of high call volumes are received, also affected areas where medical coverage need to be placed . 6.3 Oscar North Should Respond to ED HGH and Update the Silv er Command on the bed availably status and the queuing at ED .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 544 7. Events and Emergency Planning : 7.1 During adverse weather rain and dust, the Events and Emergency planning will provide a risk assessment . 7.2 HMCAS Events and Emergency planning Division have established the MIR Guide that sets out the roles and responsibilities of the command structure . 7.3 The Operational Plan for adverse weather must be distributed to the Silver command as a reference which is updated annually . 7.4 Events and Emergency Planning divi sion will provide additional resources. SOP 4.10.B . 8. Operations : 8.1 Operations on call will report to Silver C ommand . 8.2 Operational managers must reiterate SOP 2.1 - PERSONAL PROTECTION & SAFETY – Points 1&3. 8.3 All staff must revert to driving and responding to calls safely following SOP 4.6.C - EMERGENCY RESPONSE AND NON - EM ERGENCY DRIVING ADVERSE WEATHER CONDITIONS. 8.4 All communications must follow the chain of command.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 545 SOP 6.3 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Events & Emergency Planning Procedure 6.3 DRILL/EVENT PLANNING & MANAGEMEN T Implementation Date 1 J une 2019 Purpose • To centralise all drill and event requests received by HMCAS in order to ensure that they can be planned, resourced and managed accordingly. • To apply a systematic and uniform approach to the planning, assessment and facilitation of all drill and event requests. Responsibility ALL HMCAS DEPARTMENT S 1. HMCAS receives drill and event coverage requests from a broad range of community sectors. These include sporting bodies, schools and universities, commercial and retail entities, and industrial organisations. 2. In order to ensure that each request can be as sessed accurately, resourced appropriately and scheduled accordingly, it is essential that the Events & Emergency Planning Section are timeously informed of all Drill and Event coverage requests. It is also crucially important that all Drills and Events ar e uniformly risk assessed according to the accepted framework. 3. The centralization of all requests will

also help assure that all Drills and Events can be assessed, planned, staffed and managed from start to finish. In the event that an issue arises with a Drill or Event, it will also help assure that it can be managed rapidly and efficiently in order to preserve optimal service delivery. 3.1 Directed to Events and Emergency Planning (E&EP) Section : 3.1.1 All requests or info regarding upcoming drills/events must be i mmediately forwarded to the events and emergency planning department by email to asevent@hamad.qa . 3.1.2 In addition, if the event/drill is imminent (will occur in the next 5 days) a phone call to “ON CALL”, event and EP department must be made. 3.2 E&EP will inform all relevant HMCAS sections of upcoming events . 3.3 E&EP will contact the facilitator or organiser of the event/drill and provide confirmation of receipt of the request and solicit further information as required. 3.4 Based on the above, HMCAS will review the event request and either accept or refer coverage. 3.4.1 As required, resources will be requested from relevant HMCAS sections to facilitate coverage of the Drill/Event. 3.5 If cover age of the Drill/Event is accepted, the request will be added to t

he HMCAS E&EP Events Calendar. 3.6 E&EP will undertake a risk assessment based on the output of form AS - EP - F002 - V.2 and schedule a site meeting/visit with the Drill/Event organiser as required. Appropriate representation from all relevant HMCAS sections will be requested as required. 3.7 The output of the risk assessment will be communicated to all relevant HMCAS sections including Scheduling so that the event/drill can be quoted accordingly. 3.8 If acce pted, E&EP will prepare and complete an Operations Plan (in consultation with all relevant HMCAS sections). Once reviewed and approved it will be communicated to all applicable HMCAS sections. 3.9 In the interim, workforce planning will allocate staff and resources to cover the event.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 546 3.10 Coverage of the Drill/Event will be overseen and coordinated by E&EP. Any issues arising during the coverage of the Drill/Event will be reported by on - scene staff to E&EP Officer/ Manager for further a ction with other HMCAS Sections notified as required. See weekly HMCAS “ON CALL” list for event personnel on call. 3.11 Where required, and if warranted, E&EP will liaise with the Drill/Event Organiser to perform a post - event de - brief where involved HMCAS secti ons/representatives will be invited. 3.12 E&EP will compile the post - exercise report, SWOT report, lessons learned, and after - action report as applicable with consultation/feedback from all involved HMCAS sections. 3.12.1 Once completed, reviewed and approved, the rep ort will be shared with all HMCAS sections. 4. Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 552 SOP 7.1 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Training Department Procedure 7.1 ORIENTATION OF A LL HMCAS AMBULANCE P ARAMEDIC AND CRITICA L CARE PARAMEDIC STAFF Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that all HMCAS Staff are on - boarded appropriately and receive consistent orientation and training required by HMC and JCI . Responsibility ALL HMCAS AMBULANCE PARAMEDIC AND CRITIC AL CARE PARAMEDIC STAFF 1. Minimum Required Orientation : 1.1 This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) describes the minimum required orientation and training for all Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service (HMCAS) Ambulance Paramedic (AP) and Critical Care Paramedic (CCP) staff to be completed during the initial induction to meet HMCAS and Joint Commissions International (JCI) requirements. 2. HMC Human Resources (HR) Orientation : 2.1 Upon arrival, all new staff will initially report to HR to complete orientation procedures and documentation. Once the staff member has completed all documentation with HR, he/she will then report to the Training Department for Ambul

ance Service on - boarding and training. 3. HMCAS On - Boarding : 3.1 The new staff member will attend the following lectures: 3.1.1 Dress Code 3.1.2 Training Department Ho use Rules 3.1.3 Communications 3.2 The new staff member will be issued the following documents: 3.2.1 Ambulance Service Orientation Book (Soft copy) 3.2.2 HMCAS Standard Operating Procedures (Soft copy) 3.2.3 HMCAS Clinical Practice Guidelines (Soft copy) 3.2.4 HMCAS Clinical Procedures Ma nual (Soft copy) 3.2.5 HMCAS Ambulance Paramedic Pre - Course Workbook (Hard copy - AP staff only) 3.2.6 HMCAS Critical Care Paramedic Observational Phase Objectives (Soft copy - CCP staff only) 3.2.7 Corporate Orientation Guideline Document (Hard copy) 3.2.8 Corporate Orientation Checklist (Hard copy) 3.2.9 Facility Orientation Checklist (Hard copy) 3.2.10 Departmental Orientation Checklist (Hard copy) 3.3 The following training modules will be completed: 3.3.1 HMCAS Infection Control (or HMC e - learning module equivalent) 3.3.2 HMCAS Fire Safety (o r HMC e - learning module equivalent) 3.3.3 HMC Environmental Safety

Once the staff member is loaded onto the Scheduling Management System (SMS), the relevant documentation will then be uploaded onto their electronic profile. 3.7 The Training Department will liaise with the Uniform Section to get the staff member their uniform and operational equipment. 3.8 The Training Department will provide direction and support to new staff applying for their temporary and permanent DHP license. 4. Corpo rate, Facility and Departmental/Job Specific Orientation Checklists : 4.1 The Training Department will assist the new AP and

CCP staff with completion of: 4.1.1 HMC Corporate Orientation Checklist 4.1.2 Facility Orientation Checklist 4.1.3 Departmental/Job Specific Orientation C hecklist 5. Clinical Orientation and Initial Training : 5.1 The training department will provide an orientation for all newly hired Critical Care Paramedics based on their role within 999 ground operations reviewing their clinical skills and scope of practice. 5.2 The training department will provide an Ambulance Paramedic Course for all newly hired Ambulance Paramedics who require the course to obtain licensure with the Department of Healthcare Professions. 5.3 The clinical orientation will include, but is not limited to, the minimum courses offered by the Ambulance Service required for DHP licensure. 5.3.1 Ambulance Service Resuscitation Course 5.3.2 Ambulance Service Trauma Course

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 555 SOP 7.2 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Operations Procedure 7.2 ANNUAL TRAINING REQUIREMENTS AND MIN IMUM PATIENT FACING HOURS FOR AMBULANCE PARAMEDICS AND CRITI CAL CARE PARAMEDICS Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that all HMCAS clinical staff comply with CPD requirements as outlined by DHP , JCI, HMCAS. Responsibility ALL HMCAS CLINICAL S TAFF 1. Introduction : 1.1 In order to maintain clinical privileges within Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service (HMCAS), all Ambulance Paramedic (AP), Clinical Care Paramedic (CCP) staff will be required to meet minimum annual training requirements. 1.2 The minimum standards are a combination of those requirements set forth by the Department of Healthcare Profes sions (DHP), Joint Commission International (JCI), and the HMCAS executive management team. 2. Definitions: 2.1 Continuous Professional Development (CPD): In the context of HMCAS this is any training, course, or learning activity which meets the standards of DHP and is accredited or accepted by the DHP for CPD hours. 2.2 Mandatory

HMCAS CPD: In alignment with HMC CPD’s mission, our Training Department will strategically developed quarterly mandatory CPD activities to include but are not limited to; Medical, Trauma, Re suscitation, MIR, Paediatric and Obstetric and Gynecological emergencies etc. 3. DHP Minimum Requirements for Ambulance Paramedics and Critical Care Paramedics: 3.1 Resuscitation (CPR) Course every 2 years – HMCAS Resuscitation CPD meets this requirement. This activity will be available to attend 1 quarter each year. 3.2 Trauma Emergencies Course every 2 years – HMCAS Trauma CPD meets this requirement. This activity will be available to attend 1 quarter each year. 3.3 Paediatric Course – HMCAS Paediatric CPD does NOT meet this requirement. This requirement is met through completion of APLS/PALS for CCPs or PLS course for APs or equivalent courses as accepted by DHP. These cou rses require renewal upon certificate expiration. 3.4 Patient facing hours – minimum of 24 patient facing hours (2 clinical shifts) per month, with attendance averaged over 12 months. HMCAS deem the following areas of clinical practice as being patient facing for maintenance of clinical hours: 3.4.1 Any 12 - hour allocate

d shift within 999, SAS, Retrieval, or within Special Operations. 3.4.2 Event coverage 3.4.3 A shift allocated to a hospital department. 3.4.4 A shift as the Clinical Command Emergency or Clinical Command Retrieval (CCP only) based within the National Command Centre. 3.4.5 A shift as the Flight Coordinator (CCA only) within the National Command Center.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 556 3.4.6 On - call responsibilities for the provision of clinical advice are applicable exclusively to Silver clinical on - call duties. 3.5 Continuous Professional Development hours – 40 hours per year. 20 Category 1 and 20 category 2/3. 4. HMCAS Minimum Requirements: 4.1 All AP and CCP staff must meet all requirements described in section 1 through 3. 4.2 AP licensed staff: Completion of quarterly mandatory clinical HMCAS CPD activities offered by the HMCAS Training Department with the addition of the Airway CPD and MIR CPD activi ties once per year. (Total of 6 activities annually). 4.3 CCP licensed staff and Critical Care Assistant (CCA): Completion of all 4 mandatory HMCAS CPD activities offered by the HMCAS Training Department per year (1 per quarter) with the addition of the Airwa y CPD which will run once a week throughout the year, as well as, the MIR CPD once in the year .

Responsibility ALL HMCAS STAFF 1. Mandated Safety Courses : 1.1 All HMCAS staff are required to complete and maintain certification of the following online HMCAS Specific Workplace Safety Courses: 1.1.1 As part of initial orientation all HMCAS staff must complete the following courses: 1.1.1.1 Ambulance Serv ice Fire Safety Training 1.1.1.2 Ambulance Service Infection Control Training 1.1.1.3 Ambulance Service Fall Safety Training 1.1.1.4 Ambulance Service Quality Improvement and Patient Safety Training 1.1.1.5 HMC Environmental Safety eLearning Module 1.1.1.6 HMC Major Incidents eLearning Module 1.1.1.7 HM C Infection Prevention and Control Orientation eLearning module 1.1.1.8 HMC Fire Safety eLearning module 1.1.2 Recertification periods for all HMCAS Staff: 1.1.2.1 Annu

ally: 1.1.2.1.1 Ambulance Service Fire Safety (or HMC equivalent) 1.1.2.1.2 Ambulance Service Fall Safety 1.1.2.1.3 HMC Environmental Safet y 1.1.2.1.4 HMC Major Incidents 1.1.2.2 Every 2 years: 1.1.2.2.1 Ambulance Service Infection Control (or HMC equivalent). 1.1.2.3 Every 3 years: 1.1.2.3.1 For non - clinical staff, Infection Control should be completed every 3 years. 2. Accessing the HMCAS specific Safety Training Courses : 2.1 Ambulance Service training modules as well as HMC modules will be hosted on the eLearning platform, eTaleem. 2.2 Staff will receive an automated email alert from the SMS system at 30 days, 15 days and 7 days prior to expiry of the module. Therefore, staff mus t ensure they have access to their HMC email and ensure their inbox is not full. 3. Compliance : 3.1 It is the responsibility of the individual staff member to ensure they remain compliant with the patient safety courses at the intervals defined in section 1.1.2.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 558 3.2 Failure to maintain compliance may result in staff being removed from clinical duties until such time as the courses are completed. 3.3 All eTaleem course completion certificates must be sent to ASTraining@hamad.qa by email to ensure compliance is updated . Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 559 SOP 7.4 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Staff Procedure 7.4 REPORTING OF COMPLIANCE WITH MAND ATORY CLINICAL TRAIN ING Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that all HMCAS staff comply with Workplace Safety Training as mandated by Ambulance Service and Corporate Policy . Responsibility HMCAS TRAINING DEPARTMENT 1. Reporting Frequency : 1.1 A compliance report will be provided to the HMCAS Head of Professions during the first week of each new quarter. 2. Report Content: 2.1 Percentage of staff who are compliant with each of the HMCAS Mandatory Continuing Professional Development clinical training modules. 2.2 Percentage of staff who are compliant with the HMCAS offered safety courses: 2.2.1 Fire Safety 2.2.2 Infection Control 2.2.3 QPS Training 2.2.4 Fall Safety Training 2.3 Environmental Safety will be reported quarterly by the Environm ental Safety Officer within HMC’s office of Occupational Health and Safety Department. 3. Validation of Clinical Training Compliance Reports : 3.1 Completion of training in Trauma, Resuscitation, and Paed

iatric emergency care is independently verified by the DHP. 3.2 Proof of completion must be provided to the DHP for initial licensure and every 2 years thereafter for re - licensure. 3.3 Continuing Professional Development courses are accredited by the DHP. Signed attendance records and course content are maintained, as requ ired by DHP, for review and verification .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 560 SOP 7.5 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Staff Procedure 7.5 SCHEDULING OF CO RE HMCAS CPD COURSES FOR APS AND CCPS Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that staff are scheduled to attend mandatory training courses within their standard paid hours. Responsibility ALL HMCAS AMBULANCE PARAMEDICS AND CRITI CAL CARE PARAMEDIC STAFF 1. Scheduling of CPD sessions : 1.1 The Workforce Management Department is responsible for scheduling staff on the Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service (HMCAS) Continuous Professional Development (CPD) activities, ensuring alignment with operational schedule and to avoid and conflicts. 1.2 Each department within the Ambulance Service (AS) should maintain a member within the department to act as a liaison between his/her department, the Training Department and the Scheduling Department with regards to the scheduling of their staf f to CPD activities. 1.3 AP Staff should be scheduled for each quarterly CPD activity plus an MIR CPD activity once per year, ensuring all staff attend 5 CPD activities annually as

part of their annual shift requirements. CCP and CCA staff should be scheduled for each quarterly CPD activity plus an MIR and airway CPD activity once per year, ensuring all staff attend 6 CPD activities annually, with 5 CPD activities as part of their annual shift requirements, with 1 activity as overtime. 1.4 It is the respo nsibility of the supervisors and managers within the department to ensure their staff are allotted time within their mandatory hours to attend the core CPD events. 1.5 Staff should monitor their schedule and ensure that they access the online content on eTalee m and complete all activities at least 1 week prior to attending the course. 1.6 The Training Department will release a quarterly CPD schedule to all departments. 1.7 All scheduling of CPD activities should be done by the Scheduling Department and an updated sched ule of events should be sent to the Training Department. 1.8 Any queries regarding the scheduling of Training sessions should be taken up with the staff member’s department liaison or the Workforce Management Department directly. 1.9 All staff should be scheduled for the CPD event a minimum of 1 week prior to the event taking place. 2. Mandat

ed CPD Training: 2.1 Each clinical staff member must attend all mandated quarterly CPD activities. 3. Cancellation of Attendance on a Core CPD Event: 3.1 The HMCAS mandatory CPD activities a re considered as any other normally assigned duty, failure to attend is the same as failing to attend a regular duty shift. All relevant procedures for attendance, cancellation or sick leave are to be followed. 3.2 Staff are not permitted to cancel their atten dance on a HMCAS Core CPD event unless the cancelation has been approved by their supervisor or manager. The supervisor or manager is responsible for contacting the Scheduling Department to notify of a staff member’s cancelation of attendance .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 561 SOP 7.6 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Staff Procedure 7.6 DRIVER TRAINING Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that all HMCAS staff comply with required Training prior to driving any HMCAS vehicle . Responsibility ALL HMCAS STAFF 1. Minimum Requirement for Operating an HMCAS Emergency Vehicle : 1.1 Valid Qatari Driver’s License 1.2 Safe Driving Orientation 2. Non - Emergency Patient Transport Drivers : 2.1 Defined by marked/unmarked vehicles transporting HMCAS patients without emergency lights or sirens. 2.2 Staff must complete a Priority 2 (non - emergency response) driving assessment conducted by the HMCAS Training Department. 2.3 If a staff member is not successful on the Pri ority 2 driver training course due to a lack of basic driving skills, they will be requested to gain more experience driving in their personal vehicle and will be evaluated after a set interval of time, usually 6 months. 3. Emergency Patient Transport Driver : 3.1 Defined by marked/unmarked vehicles transporting HMCAS staff/patients with use of emergency light

s or sirens. 3.2 Staff must successfully complete the Priority 1 (emergency response) driving course conducted by the HMCAS Training Department. 3.3 The duration of t he Priority 1 Driving Course may be adjusted based on the individual’s performance during his/her assessment as well as prior Priority 1 driving experience. 3.4 Only staff with sufficient past driving experience and basic driving ability are eligible to attend the Priority 1 driving course. This determination is at the sole discretion of the Training Department. 4. Record of Valid Driver’s License : 4.1 Staff are required to provide the Training Department with an updated copy of his/her valid Qatar Driver’s License. C opies should be sent to AStraining@hamad.qa with his/her corporation number and “Driving License” in the subject line. 5. Break in Service over 6 months : 5.1 Staff that have not driven priority 1 for over 6 months will be required to attend a P1 driving refresher program of a minimum of 2 days .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 562 SOP 7.7 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Staff Procedure 7.7 RETURN TO CLINICAL PRACTICE FO LLOWING A BREAK IN S ERVICE Implementation Date 10 August 2017 Purpose To ensure that all HMCAS clinical staff attend an appropriate refresher training program and his/her clinical competency is appropriately assessed following a break in service . Responsibility ALL HMCAS AMBULANCE PARAMEDIC AND CRITIC AL CARE PARAMEDIC STAFF 1. Minimum Patient Facing Requirements : 1.1 Staff licensed with the Department of Healthcare Professions (DHP) as an Ambulance Paramedic (AP) or Critical Care Paramedic (CCP) are required to perform a minimum of 2 shifts (12 hours each) per month in a “patient facing” role. 1.2 Patient facing hours may include, but are not limited to, shifts on Operational units (EAS or SAS), Retrieval Services, Special Operations, event coverage, Clinical Command Emergency, Clinical Command Retrievals Flight Co - Ordinator or in - hospital shifts. 1.3 Clinical on - call roles are considered as being ‘patient facing’. 2. Break in Service : 2.1 Any AP or CC

P staff member who fails to meet the minimum r equirement of 24 hours per month for a period greater than 3 months will be considered to have had a break in service. 2.2 Following a break in service the practitioner must undergo a defined training program delivered by the HMCAS Training Department prior to returning to independent clinical duties. 2.3 This program will have the outcome of preparing the practitioner for either independent clinical practice or supervised clinical practice. 2.4 Staff moving from Scheduled Ambulance Service to Emergency Ambulance Servi ce will also undergo the 2 - day refresher program. 2.5 In addition to staff demonstrating clinical competency, they must also be current in all mandatory training programs (as described in SOP 7.4 ) prior to returning to clinical dutie s. 3. Clinical Practice Definitions : 3.1 Independent Clinical Practice: To practice as a primary member of a response unit and be able to deliver all care and to make all treatment decisions within the appropriate scope of practice without supervision. 3.2 Supervised Clinical Practice: To practice as a non - essential member of a response unit and only performing clinical interventions a

nd making treatment decisions under the supervision of an Ambulance Paramedic Educator (APEd), CCP Preceptor, or senior clinical leader . 3.3 Observational Practice: To practice as a non - essential member of a response unit while observing the clinical practice of the primary crew. If the staff member is licensed and in good standing with the DHP and HMCAS they may assist with non - invasive pati ent care at the discretion of the primary crew. 4. Requirements for Returning to Independent Clinical Practice : 4.1 Break in Service 3 to 6 months: 4.1.1 CCP Refresher Course

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 563 4.1.1.1 Total duration: 4 days subject to the individual’s training needs. 4.1.1.2 Topics and associated skills: 4.1.1.2.1 Resuscitation 4.1.1.2.2 Trauma 4.1.1.2.3 Paediatrics 4.1.1.2.4 RSI skill drill 4.1.1.2.5 Failed and difficult airway drill 4.1.2 AP Refresher Course 4.1.2.1 Total duration: 2 days 4.1.2.2 Topics and associated skills: 4.1.2.2.1 Resuscitation 4.1.2.2.2 Trauma 4.1.2.2.3 Paediatrics 4.1.2.2.4 Primary Patient Assessment 4.1.2.2.5 Airway Management 4.1.2.3 If the clinician is unsuccessful during the refresher training an action plan will be drawn up by the HMCAS Training Department that is agreed with the clinician 4.2 Break in service 6 to 12 months 4.2.1 CCP Refresher Course 4.2.1.1 Total duration: 5 days 4.2.1.2 Topics and associated skills: 4.2.1.2.1 Resuscitation 4.2.1.2.2 Trauma 4.2.1.2.3 Paediatrics 4.2.1.2.4 RSI skill drill 4.2.1.2.5 Failed and difficult airway drill 4.2.1.2.6 Anaesthetic attachment: 1 shift (8 hours – covering airway management and anaesthetic medications) 4.2.1.3 Clinical Shifts: A minimum of 2 shifts in a high activity hub with a preceptor. 4.2.2 AP Refresher Course 4.2.2.1
Total duration: 5 days 4.2.2.2 Topics and associated skills: 4.2.2.2.1 Resuscitation 4.2.2.2.2 Trauma 4.2.2.2.3 Paediatrics 4.2.2.2.4 Primary Patient Assessment

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 564 4.2.2.2.5 Airway Management 4.2.2.2.6 Clinical documentation 4.2.2.3 Clinical Shifts: A minimum of 2 shifts in a high activity hub wi th an APED. 4.2.3 If the clinician is unsuccessful during the refresher training an action plan will be drawn up by the HMCAS Training Department that is agreed upon by the clinician. 4.3 Break in Service 12 to 24 months: 4.3.1 CCP Refresher Course: 4.3.1.1 Total duration: 11 day s 4.3.1.2 Complete mandated CPD modules. 4.3.1.3 Skills to be covered: 4.3.1.3.1 RSI simple and complex 4.3.1.3.2 Failed airway 4.3.1.3.3 Difficult airway 4.3.1.3.4 Pacing and cardioversion 4.3.1.3.5 Procedural sedation 4.3.1.3.6 Normal and complicated delivery 4.3.1.4 Assessment: 1 day (completed by a Training Officer prior to conducting clinical shifts). 4.3.1.5 Anaesthetic attachment: 1 shift (cover airway management and anaesthetic medication). 4.3.1.6 Clinical shifts: Minimum of 4 shifts in a high activity hub with a preceptor. 4.3.1.7 Additional supervised patient contact can be mandated if a need is identified during initial classroom training/preceptor shifts. 4.3.1.8 Final sign off interview with eithe

r the Head of Professions or his/her designate. 4.3.2 AP Refresher Course: 4.3.2.1 Total duration: 10 days 4.3.2.2 Complete the mandated CPD modules (or equivalent) and an obst etrics review session. 4.3.2.3 Assessment: 1 day (completed by a Training Officer prior to conducting clinical shifts). 4.3.2.4 Clinical shifts: Minimum of 4 shifts in a high activity hub with a preceptor. 4.3.2.5 Additional supervised patient contact can be mandated if a need is identified during initial classroom training/preceptor shifts. 4.3.2.6 Final sign off interview with either the Head of Training or his/her designate. 4.3.3 If the clinician is unsuccessful during the refresher training an action plan will be drawn up by the HMCAS Trai ning Department that is agreed upon by the clinician. 4.4 Break in service greater than 24 Months:

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 565 4.4.1 A CCP or AP that has not completed regular clinical shifts for more than 24 months is mandated by DHP to complete a minimum of 3 months of supervised clinical pr actice prior to returning to independent clinical practice . 4.4.2 Such staff are required to complete the following prior to return to independent clinical practice: 4.4.2.1 Demonstrate all clinical induction training competencies through theory assessments, skills asse ssments, and simulations commensurate with their level of licensure (AP or CCP) followed by; 4.4.2.2 3 months or equivalent number of shifts (minimum of 48 shifts) of supervised clinical practice with a preceptor or Ambulance Paramedic Educator. 4.4.3 On the completion of all supervised shifts the AP or CCP will undergo a final assessment by either an APED or CCP preceptor. 4.4.4 On completion of final assessment all APs will undergo a final sign off interview with the Head of Training or his/her designate and all CCPs will undergo a final sign off interview with the Head of Professions or his/her designate. 5. Requirements for Returning to Observed or Supervised Clinical Practice : 5.1

Staff who have had a break in service for greater than 3 months but who will not be returning to independent clinical practice and will not serve as a primary member of the crew are required to undergo a 1 - day refresher training program covering: 5.1.1 Equipment review 5.1.2 Safe lifting and moving 5.1.3 Seatbelt use 5.1.4 Reporting process 5.1.5 Assisting in resuscitation 6. Oth er Mandatory Training : 6.1 If staff are not current with their mandatory safety training courses (i.e. fire safety, infection control, etc.) more time may be added to complete these trainings. 6.2 Prior to signing off the staff member as being able to return to cl inical duties, the training department will ensure the staff member is current with all mandatory trainings. 7. Training Priority : 7.1 The training department will offer refresher training in such a way as not to interfere with ongoing primary AP/CCP induction tr aining and/or CPD training. 7.2 Priority for refresher training will be given to those staff returning to a full - time clinical role. 7.3 Refresher training will be offered dependent on having enough training instructors, venues, and equipment available to conduct the training .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 566 SOP 7.9 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector Ambulance Service Operations Procedure 7.9 MAINTAINING LICE NSURE WITH DHP AND PRIVILEGING Implementation Date 06 July 2021 Purpose Outline the responsibilities of all D HP licensed HMCAS in maintaining their license . Responsibility ALL HMC AMBULANCE SE RVICE GROUP LICENSED , PHYSICIAN, NURSES, AMBULANCE PARAMEDICS , CRITICAL CARE PARA MEDICS, OR EMERGENCY MEDICAL DI SPATCHERS 1. Introduction : 1.1 The retention of a Department of Healthcare Professions (DHP) license is required for ALL Ambulance Service Group staff holding a post where they are listed on ORACLE as being in a clinical role or where holding a DHP license is mandated by t heir job description. 1.2 Retention of a license is the responsibility of the individual staff member and failure to retain a license (without prior agreement from your Executive Director) will result in removal from patient facing duties and could result in d isciplinary action. Failure to maintain a license will result in a change of terms and conditions of employment and may result in terminati

on of employment. 1.3 ALL staff are required to ensure that they have completed all relicensing requirements and have upl oaded all relevant/required information to DHP NO LATER than 60 days prior to the date of license expiry when staff are completing the 5th year of the license cycle. 1.4 ALL staff are required to ensure that they have completed all relicensing requirements and have uploaded all relevant/required information to DHP NO LATER than 30 days prior to the date of license expiry when staff are completing any other year of the license cycle. 1.5 ALL staff are required to ensure that their DHP registered email account AND their HMC account are checked regularly for licensing, CPD or safety bulletin updates. HMCAS staff are required to manage their email ‘in box’ to ensure that email is rece ived and responded to in a timely manner. 1.6 It is advisable that staff avoid taking holiday, during the relicensing period, in case DHP return their application and they are therefore unable to action any requests. Those taking leave during this period do so at their own risk. 2. Training and appraisal : 2.1 The minimum training and education requirements expected of HMC licensed par

amedics and CCPs is outlined in SOP 7.2 and 7.3 . 2.2 The minimum training and education requirements expected of an HMC licensed ambulance Emergency Medical Dispatcher is outlined in SOP 3.29 . 2.3 Staff are reminded that it is their responsibility to ensure that they are fully aw are of DHP requirements regarding relicensure. These requirements are published on the DHP website. 2.3.1 To support staff HMCAS maintains a list of essential licensures and relicensure requirements published under the HMC e - learning platform etaleem. This infor mation is for guidance only and does not superseded any specific information circulated by DHP. Staff are directed to point 2.3 of this SOP. 2.3.2 Staff are required to ensure that they can demonstrate DHP CPD cycle compliance as well as DHP required CPR courses as per scope of practice during their annual appraisal. Failure to be able to demonstrate compliance may will result in an appraisal

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 567 score of no higher than THREE . A subsequent action plan to demonstrate a process to complete will be required. 3. Action to b e taken if DHP license is delayed (Please refer to Policy HR 3009): 3.1 Staff are reminded of point 1.3 of this SOP. 3.2 If a staff member has not received their new license within 14 days of when the license expires then the following is to be instigated: 3.2.1 Line ma nager is to be informed IN PERSON AND VIA EMAIL 3.2.1.1 Line manager will ensure that: 3.2.1.1.1 The staff member is not rostered on a clinical shift from 00.01am on the day the license expires. 3.2.1.1.2 Staff will be placed onto UNPAID leave until which time their license is renewe d. 3.2.1.1.3 The case will be reviewed, and a written warning will be issued and an annual appraisal score of a maximum of 2 will be applied REGARDLESS of overall performance within the appraisal period. 3.2.1.1.4 Staff who fail to license for a 2nd time will be referred for disciplinary at Executive Director level. 3.2.2 The Training Department is to be informed IN PERSON AND VIA EMAIL. 3.3 Support from the Training Department with relicensure iss

ues: 3.3.1 The Training Department’s responsibilities to support staff licensure cease after the initial permanent license is issued. 3.3.2 Any request for assistance with re licensure can only originate from an Operations Manager or higher. Individual staff are not permitted to seek assistance without the authority of their Operations Manager, Senior Oper ations Manager, Assistant Executive Director or Executive Director otherwise such requests will be denied. 4. Privileging : 4.1 This SOP is a supportive document produced as part of the HMC AS privileging policy, HMCAS Clinical Practice Guidelines, and HMCAS Clinical Procedures Manual. 4.2 All staff who are licensed with DHP either as an Ambulance Paramedic (AP), Critical Care Paramedic (CCP), or Ambulance Service Medical Dispatcher (ASMD) are privileged via the applicable Head of Professions, through the office o f the Group Medical Director. This is regardless of their area of employment within HMCAS as all licensed Ambulance Paramedics, Critical Care Paramedics, and ASMDs are professionally accountable to the office of the applicable Head of Professions. 4.3 The degr ee of privileging is related to the level at which the clini

cian is licensed with DHP and their anticipated exposure to managing patients. Privileging is a patient safety requirement and does not affect an individual’s terms and conditions of employment. 4.4 T he individuals historical training and/or previous level of privileging is not a factor in the level of privileging that will be awarded. as outlined in point 4.3. 4.5 HMCAS staff are not entitled to function outside of their level of privileging except during a major incident on the instruction of Silver Clinical, Silver Command, Group Medical Director, or Gold Command.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 568 4.6 HMCAS will operate four levels of privileging for DHP registered AP and CCP staff. 4.6.1 Level 4 (CCP only) 4.6.1.1 CCP’s working in essential support roles where patient facing roles are limited will have a reduced scope operating at level 3. The additional authority to administer opiate analgesia, Ketamine analgesia (but not Ketamine sedation) and Midazolam for the control of seizures but not sedation will be reviewed on a case per case basis 4.6.1.2 Level 3 (reflecting Ambulance Paramedic as per DHP scope) 4.6.1.3 Level 2 (reflecting Basic Paramedic as per DHP scope) 4.6.1.4 Level 1 is at the first responder level of care 4.7 Regardless of the level of privileging, awarded to an HMC AS clinician, they must retain their license as per their Job title/description (either as an Ambulance Paramedic, Critical Care Paramedic, or ASMD) to meet the terms and conditions of their employment. Unless continued licensure with DHP is no longer a requirement of their continued employment .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 572 SOP 8.4 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services Clinical Desk Procedure 8.4 CLINICAL DESK PROCES S FLOWS Implementation Date 0 6 October 202 0 Purpose To organize and standardize the utilization of process flows for routine processes used on the Clinical Desk . Responsibility ALL STAFF ASSIGNED T O THE CLINICAL DESK 1. Background and Purpose : 1.1 In undertaking daily operational duties, staff on the Clinical Desk perform tasks that are of a routine nature and interlink with other routine tasks to form routine processes. With the objective of improving standardization of the se processes the Clinical Desk shall utilize process flows. 2. Definitions applicable within this SOP : 2.1 ‘CAD’ shall mean Computer Aided Dispatch and applies to the use of any approved and officially implemented Computer Aided dispatch system used by HMC Ambulance Service including, but not limited to, Najem (NJM) or Valencia. 2.2 ‘CC: Emergency’ or 'Clinical Coordina tor: Emergency' refers to the Critical Care Paramedic employed by HMC Ambulance Service with level 4 clinical privi

leging and deployed to the Clinical Desk to provide clinical support and hospital pre - arrival notifications for patient facing staff. 2.3 ‘CC: Re trieval’ or 'Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval' refers to the Critical Care Paramedic employed by HMC Ambulance Service with level 4 clinical privileging and deployed to the Clinical Desk to provide clinical support, transfer triage and coordinate internatio nal aeromedical cases. 2.4 ‘Clinical Desk’ (the desk) means the division under the governance of Clinical Services that includes the Clinical Coordinator: Emergency, Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval, LifeFlight Coordinator, EMD: Retrievals Coordinators within the HMC Ambulance Services Control and Communications Center under the leadership of the SME: Clinical Desk. 2.5 ‘Process Flow’ means the graphical representation of the steps or phases and the sequence in which they are undertaken when performing a given rout ine procedure. 3. Procedure : 3.1 The Clinical Desk employs the use of process flows to specify the standard approach to routine processes undertaken in daily operations of the Clinical Desk. 3.1.1 These process flows may be set out without an associated SOP unless the proce

ss is of a nature that requires a written, detailed SOP. 3.2 The authorization of Process Flow implementation will be at the discretion of the Assistant Executive Director of Clinical Services together with the Assistant Executive Director of any other di vision affected by the Process Flow. 3.2.1 Where approved for implementation without executive resolution, the process flow shall be signed off by the Assistant Executive Directors authorizing its implementation before being published. 3.2.2 Where deemed necessary, th e process flow will be submitted for executive resolution through the standard HMC Ambulance Services resolution process.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 573 3.3 Process flows that have received resolution for implementation and do not have an associated SOP shall be appended to this SOP and num bered accordingly. 3.4 Deviation from process flows: 3.4.1 A Clinical Coordinator on duty at the Clinical Desk as a CC: Emergency or CC: Retrieval may deviate from a process flow only when considered reasonably necessary to provide clinical benefit to the patient. 3.4.2 Notwithstanding 3.4.1 above, any deviation from a resoluted process flow must be authorized by a senior manager or on - call commander. 3.4.3 Any deviation from a process flow, by any person, shall be documented in the appropriate CAD system case notes and include a reason for the deviation .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 574 SOP 8.5 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services Clinical Desk Procedure 8. 5 CLINICAL DESK ONBOARDING AND CURRENCY Implementation Date 20 April 20 22 Purpose To set out the standard onboarding and currency standards for staff assigned to duties on the Clinical Desk . Responsibility ALL STAFF ASSIGNED T O THE CLINICAL DESK 1. Background and Purpose : 1.1 The purpose of this document is to establish and clarify the requirements and process for the onboarding of new staff and ongoing currency for all staff assigned to a role on the Clinical Desk. 1.2 This SOP sets out: 1.2.1 Onboarding standards for newly assigned staff to the Clinical Desk. 1.2.2 Clinical Desk currency standards 1.2.3 Re - onboarding standards for those staff that have not maintained currency in accordance with provisions set out below. 2. Definitions applicable within this SOP : 2.1 ‘Clinical Desk’ , (the desk) means the division under the governance of Clinica l Services that includes the Clinical Coordinator: Emergency, Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval, LifeFlight Coordinator, EMD: Retrie

vals Coordinators within the HMC Ambulance Services Control and Communications Center under the leadership of the SME: Clinica l Desk. 2.2 ‘Clinical Coordination’ means the combined roles of the CC: Emergency and CC: Retrieval who are collectively responsible for delivering clinical coordination and support for HMC Ambulance Services. 2.3 ‘LifeFlight Coordinator’ means the combined roles of the CC: Emergency and CC: Retrieval who are collectively responsible for delivering clinical coordination and support for HMC Ambulance Services. 2.4 ‘EMD: Retrievals Coordinator’ refers to the Emergency Medical Dispatcher deployed to the Clinical Desk to d ispatch, control and coordinate Retrieval resources and missions. 2.5 ‘MPDS’ means Medical Priority Dispatch System, sometimes referred to as the Advanced Medical Priority Dispatch System (AMPDS), the system used in HMC Ambulance Service communications centers for structured, standardized and systematic call interrogation and dispatch of appropriate resources as well as standardized provision of pre - arrival medical instructions. 3. Aims of Currency and Orientation : 3.1 Currency standards and orientation aim to ensure the maintenance of bus

iness continuity regarding: 3.1.1 Equipping newly assigned staff with the knowledge and skills required for their role on the desk, ensuring they feel prepared to meet the daily demands imposed on them. 3.1.2 Minimizing adverse events that may im pact the quality of clinical care received by HMC Ambulance Service patients. 3.1.3 Reduction of risks posed to aeromedical missions through ensuring continuous and consistent coordination and tracking of a high standard.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 575 3.1.4 Ensuring professional and continual prov ision of quality service delivery at a high standard. 3.1.5 Ensure the maintenance of business continuity through appropriate deployment of skilled well - trained staff. 4. Staff Assignment to the Clinical Desk : 4.1 Deployment on the Clinical Desk is a requirement for al l consoles (CC: Emergency, CC: Retrieval, LifeFlight Coordinator, EMD: Retrievals Coordinator) to ensure business continuity and sustainable provision of quality service. 4.2 Staffing of Clinical Coordination and LifeFlight Coordination is provided by Emergenc y Operations, Retrieval and Lifeflight sections of Clinical Services, and the EMD: Retrievals Coordinator role is staffed by the Healthcare Coordination Centre. 4.3 Staff assignment to roles in the Clinical Desk must comply with the following requirements: 4.3.1 CC: Emergency staff must be a clinically privileged CCP (Level 4) deployed in Emergency Operations and/or LifeFlight with a minimum accumulative time in Charlie/LifeFlight of 6 - months. 4.3.2 CC: Retrieval staff must be a clinically privileged CCP (Level 4) deployed in a role undertaking l

ocal and international Retrievals with a minimum accumulative time in Retrievals of 6 - months and have completed a minimum of 2 international retrievals. 4.3.3 LifeFlight Coordinator staff must be a clinically privileged AP (Level 3) and a ppointed as a CCA in LifeFlight Operations with a minimum of 6 - months of LifeFlight Operations experience. 4.3.4 EMD: Retrievals Coordinator staff must be registered as an Emergency Medical Dispatcher and appointed in the role of dispatcher in HCC with a minimum of 6 - months HCC dispatching experience. 5. Onboarding Standards : 5.1 Clinical Coordination : 5.1.1 The onboarding of CC: Emergency and CC: Retrieval shall be carried out through a similar process and content to support cross functional performance. 5.1.2 Onboarding sh all be coordinated by the SME: Clinical Desk, or an appropriate delegated staff member, in collaboration with the relevant division management. 5.1.3 Prior to commencement of any shifts on the desk, Clinical Coordinator staff are required to complete the followi ng, unless otherwise agreed by the SME: Clinical Desk. 5.1.3.1 NCC Access approval 5.1.3.2 Either pre - shift briefing on rules and conduct in HMCAS communicat

ions centers or orientation training covering the same. 5.1.4 Prior to commencement of independent shifts on the desk, Cl inical Coordinator staff are required to complete the following, unless otherwise approved by the SME: Clinical Desk: 5.1.4.1 CC: Emergency assigned Staff 5.1.4.1.1 CD Onboarding – CC: Emergency Orientation 5.1.4.1.2 CD Onboarding – CC: Emergency Preceptorship 5.1.4.1.3 LifeNet Alert system fa miliarization

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 576 5.1.4.1.4 Cisco Telephone system and IVR familiarization 5.1.4.1.5 Any additional training agreed to by the Clinical Services management team. 5.1.4.2 CC: Retrieval assigned staff: 5.1.4.2.1 CD Onboarding – CC: Emergency Orientation 5.1.4.2.2 CD Onboarding – CC: Emergency Preceptorship (re duced length) 5.1.4.2.3 CD Onboarding – CC: Retrieval Orientation 5.1.4.2.4 CD Onboarding – CC: Retrieval Preceptorship 5.1.4.2.5 LifeNet Alert system familiarization 5.1.4.2.6 Cisco Telephone system and IVR familiarization 5.1.4.2.7 Valencia CAD Call - taker training 5.1.4.2.8 Any additional training agreed to by the Clinical Services management team. 5.1.4.3 Preceptorship shifts will usually be structured as set out below; however, remains subject to management discretion based on the staff members prior service experience, divisional assignments and operational needs : Assigned Role Primary Role Role Supporting NCC General Total CC: Emergency 2 (minimum) 2 (minimum) 1 (experience dependent) 5 CC: Retrieval 3 (minimum) 2 (minimum) 1 (experience dependent) 6 5.2 LifeFlight Coordinators . 5.2.1 The onboarding of LifeFlight Coordinat

ors will be closely supported by the LifeFlight division specifically pertaining to expert knowledge areas. 5.2.2 Onboarding shall be coordinated by the SME: Clinical Desk, or an appropriate delegated staff member, in collaborat ion with the relevant division management. 5.2.3 Prior to commencement of any shifts on the desk, LifeFlight Coordinator staff are required to complete the following, unless otherwise agreed by the SME: Clinical Desk. 5.2.3.1 NCC Access approval 5.2.3.2 Either pre - shift briefin g on rules and conduct in HMCAS communications centers or orientation training covering the same. 5.2.4 Prior to commencement of independent shifts on the desk, LifeFlight Coordinator staff are required to complete the following, unless otherwise approved by the SME: Clinical Desk: 5.2.4.1 CD Onboarding – LifeFlight Coordinator Orientation 5.2.4.2 CD Onboarding – LifeFlight Coordinator Preceptorship 5.2.4.3 Cisco Telephone system and IVR familiarization 5.2.4.4 LifeNet Alert system familiarization

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 577 5.2.4.5 Any additional training agreed to by the Clinic al Services management team. 5.2.4.6 Preceptorship shifts will usually be structured as set out below; however, remains subject to management discretion based on the staff members prior service experience, divisional assignments and operational needs . Assigned Role Primary Role Role Supporting NCC General Total LifeFlight Coordinator 4 (minimum) 2 (minimum) 1 (experience dependent) 7 5.3 EMD Retrieval Coordinator: 5.3.1 The onboarding of EMD: Retrieval Coordinator shall be carried out as a collaborative arrangement between Clinical Desk and Healthcare Coordination Centre management. 5.3.2 Prior to commencement of any shifts on the desk, EMD: Retrieval Coordinator staff are required to complete the following, unless otherwise agreed between Clinical Desk and Hea lthcare Coordination Centre management. 5.3.2.1 NCC Access approval 5.3.2.2 Either pre - shift briefing on rules and conduct in HMCAS communications centers or orientation training covering the same. 5.3.3 Prior to commencement of independent shifts on the desk, EMD: Retrieval Co ordinator staff are requi

red to complete the following, unless otherwise agreed between Clinical Desk and Healthcare Coordination Centre management. 5.3.3.1 CD Onboarding – EMD: Retrieval Coordinator Orientation 5.3.3.2 CD Onboarding – EMD: Retrieval Coordinator Preceptorship 5.3.3.3 Cisco Telephone system training (facilitated by HCC) 5.3.3.4 Valencia CAD Training (facilitated by HCC) 5.3.3.5 Any additional training agreed to by the Clinical Desk and Healthcare Coordination Centre management. 5.3.3.6 Preceptorship shifts will usuall y be structured as set out below; however, remains subject to management discretion based on the staff members prior service experience, divisional requirements and operational needs . Assigned Role Sierra Retrieval/Similar Shifts – focus: Ground Operation s (Supervised) Shifts – focus: Aeromedical Operations (Supervised) Total EMD: Retrieval Coordinator 2 8 (minimum) 8 (minimum) 18 6. Currency Standards . 6.1 All staff assigned to the desk are required to retain the following minimum currency requirements. 6.1.1 CC: Emergency assigned staff will be considered current for duties on the desk if the following are met: 6.1.1.1 Hold currency as a Charlie or LifeFlight CCP in Emerg
ency Operations or LifeFlight.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 578 6.1.1.2 Has completed Clinical Desk onboarding. 6.1.1.3 Has completed a minimum of four (4) Clinical Desk shifts within the preceding two (2) calendar months. 6.1.1.4 Has completed the annual CC Governing Document Review. 6.1.1.5 Holds current access to: 6.1.1.5.1 The Clinical Desk share drive folder “hmcfs03 \ Clinical Command Desk”. 6.1.1.5.2 NCC Acc ess approval 6.1.1.5.3 Credentialing and access to required information systems. 6.1.1.6 Any other requirements agreed to by management. 6.1.2 CC: Retrieval assigned staff will be considered current for duties on the desk if the following are met: 6.1.2.1 Holds currency as a Retrieval CC P in Retrieval 6.1.2.2 Has completed Clinical Desk onboarding 6.1.2.3 Has completed a minimum of four (4) Clinical Desk shifts within the preceding two (2) calendar months. 6.1.2.4 Has completed the annual CC Governing Document Review. 6.1.2.5 Holds current access to: 6.1.2.5.1 The Clinical Desk s hare drive folder “hmcfs03 \ Clinical Command Desk” 6.1.2.5.2 NCC Access approval 6.1.2.5.3 Credentialing and access to required information systems. 6.1.2.6 Any other requirements agreed to b

y management. 6.1.3 LifeFlight Coordinator assigned staff will be considered current for duties on t he desk if the following are met: 6.1.3.1 Hold currency as a LifeFlight CCA in LifeFlight. 6.1.3.2 Has completed Clinical Desk onboarding. 6.1.3.3 Has completed a minimum of four (4) Clinical Desk shifts within the preceding two (2) calendar months. 6.1.3.4 Has completed the annual LFC G overning Document Review 6.1.3.5 Holds current access to: 6.1.3.5.1 The Clinical Desk share drive folder “hmcfs03 \ Clinical Command Desk” 6.1.3.5.2 NCC Access approval 6.1.3.5.3 Credentialing and access to required information systems. 6.1.3.6 Any other requirements agreed to by management. 6.1.4 EMD: Retrieval Coordinator assigned staff will be considered current for duties on the desk if the following are met : 6.1.4.1 Holds currency as a Dispatcher in Healthcare Coordination Centre.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 579 6.1.4.2 Has Completed Clinical Desk onboarding 6.1.4.3 Has completed a minimum of four (4) Clinical Desk shifts within the preceding two (2) calendar months. 6.1.4.4 Holds current access to: 6.1.4.4.1 The Clinical Desk share drive folder “hmcfs03 \ Clinical Command Desk” 6.1.4.4.2 NCC Access approval 6.1.4.4.3 Credentialing and access to required information systems. 6.1.4.5 Any other require ments agreed to by the Clinical Services and Healthcare Coordination Centre management team. 7. Staff Re - onboarding : 7.1 Where a staff member has completed onboarding; however, has not maintained the minimum shift currency requirement, they are considered not curr ent for independent duties on the desk. 7.2 Staff that are no longer current for independent duties on the desk will be required to undertake re - onboarding in accordance to the re - onboarding matrix (below) unless otherwise agreed by the management team of the Clinical Desk and relevant staff division. 7.3 Re - onboarding shall be supported by the division from which the staff member is assigned and shall be coordinated by the SME: Clinical Desk, or an appropriate
delegated staff member, in liaison with the assigning division .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 581 SOP 8.6 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services Clinical Desk Procedure 8.6 CLINICAL DESK DA ILY OPERATIONS Implementation Date 0 6 October 202 0 Purpose To standardize and clarify daily routine shift procedures of the Clinical Desk. Responsibility ALL STAFF ASSIGNED T O THE CLINICAL DESK 1. Background and Purpose : 1.1 The Clinical Desk operates within the National Command Centre and is therefore expected to work in accordance with the general provisions of SOP 3.9 Daily Operations in the Communications Centre’s. 1.2 The Clinical Desk, ho wever, operates with a different ethos and is staffed differently to the Communication Center’s; therefore, this SOP sets out specific provisions that apply to the daily operation of the Clinical Desk. 2. Definitions applicable within this SOP : 2.1 ‘Clinical Desk’ (the desk) means the division under the governance of Clinical Services that includes the Clinical Coordinator: Emergency, Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval, LifeFlight Coordinator, EMD: Retrievals Coordinators within the HMC Ambulance Servic es Control and Com

munications Center under the leadership of the SME: Clinical Desk. 2.2 ‘CC: Emergency' or 'Clinical Coordinator: Emergency' refers to the Critical Care Paramedic employed by HMC Ambulance Service with level 4 clinical privileging and deployed to the Clinical Desk to provide clinical support and hospital pre - arrival notifications for patient facing staff. 2.3 ‘CC: Retrieval’ or 'Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval' refers to the Critical Care Paramedic employed by HMC Ambulance Service with level 4 cli nical privileging and deployed to the Clinical Desk to provide clinical support, transfer triage and coordinate international aeromedical cases. 2.4 ‘LifeFlight Coordinator’ refers to the Critical Care Assistant employed by HMC Ambulance Service with level 3 c linical privileging and deployed to the Clinical Desk to dispatch, control and coordinate LifeFlight resources and missions. 2.5 ‘EMD : Retrievals Coordinator’ refers to the Emergency Medical Dispatcher deployed to the Clinical Desk to dispatch, control and coo rdinate Retrieval resources and missions. 2.6 ‘LifeX’ refers to the Integrated Command and Control System used by NCC - TCC for radio and telephony communications to and fr

om consoles in the NCC - TCC. 3. Commencement of shift : 3.1 Clinical Desk shift timings shall be de termined by the SME: Clinical Desk in collaboration with relevant divisions and will be reviewed regularly based on operational need. 3.2 All staff assigned to the desk, regardless of specific role, shall report for duty at the National Command Centre fifteen minutes prior to the commencement of shift to facilitate endorsement between the oncoming and off - going staff. 3.3 Off - going staff shall endorse their shifts occurrences, ongoing or pending issues and cases to the oncoming shift. 3.4 Staff must undertake an operat ional check of their console and workspace at the commencement of shift including, but not limited to:

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 582 3.4.1 Confirming their HMC workstation and screens are functional and that they can access network services. 3.4.2 Login to Cisco Finesse and check that their phonel ine is functional for incoming calls, through the IVR (4439 8833), and outgoing calls. 3.4.3 Start up and login to all applications necessary to carry out their specific duties for the shift including, as a minimum and as applicable to the console, Valencia CAD, NJM, LifeX, Skytrac, LifeNet and Cerner. 4. Break management and fatigue : 4.1 Staff assigned to the Clinical Desk shall be entitled to take fatigue management breaks as needed within accordance to the below set out conditions. 4.2 At any given time, the below shall apply: 4.2.1 Where two Clinical Coordinators are on duty, at least one Clinical Coordinator shall be present at the desk at all times. 4.2.1.1 Where only one Clinical Coordinator is on duty, either the Clinical Coordinator or LifeFlight Coordinator must be present at th e desk at all times. 4.2.2 Either the LifeFlight Coordinator or CC: Emergency shall be present at the desk at all times. 4.2.3 Where two EMD: Retrievals Coordinators ar

e on duty, at least one EMD: Retrievals Coordinator shall be present at the desk at all times. 4.2.3.1 Where only one EMD: Retrievals Coordinator is on duty, either the CC: Retrieval or EMD: Retrievals Coordinator shall be present at the desk at all times. 4.3 When taking a break, staff must notify either one of the Clinical Coordinators that they are going on a break and provide their contact number and intended break location. 4.4 Any single break taken by any staff member of the desk may not exceed 45 minutes in duration and should not be excessive in quantity through the duration of the shift. 4.5 Breaks are subject to operational demand posed on the desk, and any staff member on a break may be recalled to duty by a Clinical Coordinator, supervisor or manager should the need arise. 5. National Command Centre TCC rules : 5.1 All staff assigned to duties on the desk must be fa miliar with SOP 3.9 Daily Operations in the Communications Centre’s, including all provisions applicable to conduct within the HMCAS NCC Communications Centre. 5.1.1 Any uncertainty about what is acceptable inside the NCC - TCC should be raised to the Oscar NCC on duty at the time. 6. Handling and managemen

t of infrastructure : 6.1 All Clinical Desk staff shall use their own credentials when logging into any applications and systems including, but not limited to, NJM, LifeX, Valenci a CAD and Cerner. 6.2 Staff must ensure they log - off of all workstations at the completion of their shift prior to leaving their console including NJM, LifeX and HMC computers. 6.3 All HMC Ambulance Service workstations used on the Clinical Desk must be restarted once a night shift between the hours of 00:00 and 05:00.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 583 6.4 Staff should ensure that the computer applications required, on short notice, as part of their duties is ready for use at any time while at their console with minimal delay. 6.4.1 Workstations should be un locked and awaiting user input. 6.4.2 Required applications should remain open, logged in and ready for user input. 6.5 Staff should not store personal files on Clinical Desk workstations as these are shared operational computers that may, without notice, be sent fo r urgent upkeep and maintenance. 6.6 Multi - user HMC Ambulance Service workstations will be scheduled by the SME: Clinical Desk, in collaboration with the IT Delivery and Transformation Program, for an annual workstation rebuild and other necessary maintenance as required. 6.7 Cisco Telephone Failure : 6.7.1 Any failure involving the function of the Clinical Desk telephone system must immediately be escalated to the Oscar HCC and be handled in accordance with SOP 3.10 as guided by the Oscar HCC. 6.7.2 The following persons m ust be notified immediately: 6.7.2.1 Oscar NCC 6.7.2.2 Consultant Critical Care Paramedic: Effectiveness 6.7.2.3 SME: Clinical

Desk 6.7.3 LifeX shall be used as the primary back - up system for all outgoing phone calls until the issue is resolved. 6.8 Tetra Radio or Tetra Network Failure : 6.8.1 Any failure involving the function of any Clinical Desk tetra radio or the tetra radio system must immediately be escalated to the Oscar NCC and be handled in accordance with SOP 3.11 as guided by the Oscar NC C. 6.8.2 The following persons must be notified immediately: 6.8.2.1 Oscar HCC 6.8.2.2 Consultant Critical Care Paramedic: Effectiveness 6.8.2.3 SME: Clinical Desk 6.8.3 If the system failure is isolated to the LifeX system, staff shall use the handheld tetra radios as a fallback until LifeX function is restored. 6.9 NJM and LifeX failure : 6.9.1 In the event of any NJM or LifeX performance failure the user should attempt to restart the application or the workstation. 6.9.2 If 6.9.1 above does not resolve the issue it must be escalated to the Oscar NCC whom s hall arrange the appropriate response to resolve the issue. 6.9.3 Where the failure of NJM or ICCS performance cannot be resolved within 15 minutes, Clinical Desk staff must notify: 6.9.3.1 Consultant Critical Care Paramedic: Effectiveness 6.9.3.2 SME:
Clinical Desk 6.10 HMC worksta tion failure :

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 584 6.10.1 In the event of a failure of normal performance of the HMC Ambulance Service workstation or its accessories, the user must perform a computer restart using the restart option in the windows start menu. 6.10.2 If 6.10.1 above does not resolve the iss ue, contact HICT at 4439 1444 who shall assist remotely to resolve the issue. 6.10.3 Where the failure of HMC Workstation performance cannot be resolved within 15 - minutes, staff must notify: 6.10.3.1 Oscar HCC/HCC Utilization Team Leader (UTL) 6.10.3.2 Consultant Critical Care Par amedic: Effectiveness 6.10.3.3 SME: Clinical Desk 6.11 CareMonX (Valencia) CAD failure : 6.11.1 In the event of any CareMonX CAD performance failure the user should attempt to restart the application or the workstation. 6.11.2 If 6.11.1 above does not resolve the issue it must be escalated to the HCC PTL at 4439 0013 whom shall guide any further action. 6.11.3 Any failure of CareMonX CAD used on the Clinical Desk shall be managed in accordance with SOP 3.18 as guided by the Oscar HCC. 6.11.4 Where the failur e of CareMonX CAD performance cannot be resolved within 15 - minutes, Clinical D

esk staff must notify: 6.11.4.1 Consultant Critical Care Paramedic: Effectiveness 6.11.4.2 SME: Clinical Desk 6.12 LifeNet failure : 6.12.1 In the event of any LifeNet performance failure the user should attem pt to restart the application and the workstation. 6.12.2 Where the failure of LifeNet performance affects all Lifenet Alert stations on the Clinical Desk preventing transmission of ECGs to the Clinical Desk or to the receiving hospital, a Clinical Coordinator mu st immediately notify: 6.12.2.1 Consultant Critical Care Paramedic: Effectiveness 6.12.2.2 SME: Clinical Desk 6.12.3 If 6.12.1 above does not resolve the issue and the ‘popup’ error message requests the user to “…start the LIFENET Alert user interface with administrator privileges …” contact HICT at 4439 1444 and follow the step - by - step guidance outlined in the “Guide for the resolution of Lifenet Alert Application ‘Administrator Error” (See Figure 1 below). 6.12.4 If the troubleshooting steps in 6.12.1 and 6.12.2 do not resolve the issue it must be escalated to the SME: Clinical Desk directly for further guidance. 6.13 Skytrac or DaWinci failure : 6.13.1 In the event of any inability to access the Skytrac or DaW

inci web applications the LifeFlight Coordinator should restart their browser and/or worksta tion. 6.13.2 If 6.13.1 above does not resolve the issue and web browsing access is working, it must be immediately escalated to Oscar LifeFlight on - call. 6.13.2.1 If web browsing is not possible the LifeFlight Coordinator should contact the SME: Clinical Desk for further guidance.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 585 6.13.3 Where the failure of Skytrac or DaWinci performance cannot be resolved within 15 - minutes, the LifeFlight Coordinator must notify: 6.13.4 Operations Officer: LifeFlight 6.13.5 SME: Clinical Desk 7. Ending shift : 7.1 Staff must ensure all documentation and shift reports are completed for their shift and log - off of all workstations and systems before leaving their console at the end of every shift. 7.2 Off - going staff must complete social cleaning of their console, chair, keyboard and mouse before handing over to the o n - coming staff. 7.3 Off - going staff must provide an endorsement to the on - coming staff including, but not limited to: 7.3.1 Ongoing calls 7.3.2 Documentation of ongoing calls 7.3.3 Any major concerns or issues from their shift
