Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 587 SOP 8.7 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services: Clinical Desk Procedure 8.7 CLINICAL DESK PH ONE CALL MANAGEMENT Implementation Date 0 6 October 202 0 Purpose To set out the standards for the management of telephone systems and standardize the approach to the handling of inbound and outbound telephone calls of the Clinical Desk. Responsibility ALL STAFF ASSIGNED T O THE CLINICAL DESK 1. Background and Purpose : 1.1 The staff of the Clinical Desk handle incoming calls for case coordination or clinical consultation of both emergency and non - emergency calls, as well as conducting outbound calls for the same purposes. These calls include staff within the HMC Ambulance Service as well as external stakeholders. This SOP provides clarity and guidance to Clinical Desk staff on the standards for managing these calls as well as the use of the Cisco phone system. 2. Definitions applicable within this SOP : 2.1 ‘Cisco telephone’ refers to the specific make of telephone used on the Clinical Desk for inbound and outbound calls. 2.2 ‘Clinical Desk’ (the desk) means t

he division under the governa nce of Clinical Services that includes the Clinical Coordinator: Emergency, Clinical Coordinator: Retrieval, LifeFlight Coordinator, EMD: Retrievals Coordinators within the HMC Ambulance Services Control and Communications Center under the leadership of th e SME: Clinical Desk. 2.3 ‘IMIST - AMBO’ refers to the acronym used for patient endorsement as set - out in SOP 4.8.B . 2.4 ‘IVR’ (Interactive Voice Response), refers to the telephony technology that reads the available pre - pro grammed options to the caller, who can then select their desired choice on which the system then directs their call to the appropriate console. 3. General Provisions : 3.1 Clinical Desk staff shall only use the HMC Ambulance Service Cisco telephones for all incomi ng and outgoing calls. 3.2 All inbound and outbound calls of the Clinical Desk will be recorded for quality assurance and improvement purposes. 3.3 Clinical Desk staff must login to the Cisco Agent on their respective workstation and make their number available in the IVR immediately at the start of shift and should only log - off again at the end of shift. 3.4 Availability in the IVR must be monitored throughout t

he shift and staff should only set their number not ready (i.e. unavailable) for the following ‘not ready re asons’: 3.4.1 Meeting or training – Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Meeting” 3.4.2 Bathroom use - Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Personal” 3.4.3 Short break - Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Break” 3.4.4 Meal break - Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Meal” 3.4.5 Prayer - Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Prayer Time” 3.4.6 Leaving the console to coordinate a case directly in person with other persons in NCC - Set ‘not ready reason’ as “Standby”

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 588 3.5 EMD: Retrievals Coordinator shall continue to make themselves available and operate under the HCC (9 8555) IVR and adh ere to SOP 3.21 and SOP 3.22 . 3.5.1 Where calls are routed to EMD: Retrievals Coordinator from the Clinical Desk IVR (9 8833) they should forward the call to an appropriate person as guided w ithin this SOP. 3.5.2 Where it is not possible to forward the call, all relevant information as per section 5 below must be obtained and the appropriate person immediately notified. 3.6 All inbound and outbound calls should be conducted using appropriate phone etiqu ette. 3.6.1 Clinical Desk staff handling any inbound or outbound call should do so in a professional and courteous manner no matter the situation. 3.6.2 Use appropriate language based on the caller’s expectations, knowledge, language and cultural background. 3.6.2.1 Use appro priate medical terminology when talking with another medical professional. 3.6.2.2 Avoid words or phrases that may be viewed offensively or be ambiguous for the caller. 3.6.2.3 Avoid sounding abrupt and impatient with the caller. 3.6.3 Maintain an appropriate professional vo

cal tone and use clear concise communication. 3.6.3.1 Avoid the impression that you are rushing a caller. 3.6.3.2 Do not raise your voice in frustration or unnecessarily prolong a call. 3.6.4 Maintain control of the conversation by politely and professionally directing the caller to answer questions you ask. 3.6.5 Avoid asking a caller to repeat themselves by taking appropriate notes during the call. 3.6.6 Confirm your understanding of information received by summarizing or paraphrasing this back to the caller for their confirmation. 4. Opening t he Call : 4.1 Inbound calls must be answered as soon as available and should ideally be answered within 15 seconds. 4.2 Clinical Desk staff should answer all inbound calls professionally using the phrase, ‘_____ [your role], how may I help you?’ (e.g.: ‘CC: Emergen cy, how may I help you?’ ) as a guide. 5. Obtaining and Confirming Information : 5.1 Obtain the details and call back information for the caller, where necessary. 5.1.1 Where the caller is ambulance service staff, ask the caller to provide their name and unit call sign. 5.1.2 Where the caller is not ambulance service staff, ask the caller to provide their name, corporation numbe

r and contact number. 5.2 Obtain the patient’s unique identification, such as their QID or HC number, and a secondary confirmation identifier, such a s HC number, QID or name, whenever required. 5.3 Obtain an IMIST - AMBO endorsement as well as any additional appropriate and applicable clinical or case information.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 589 5.3.1 Where a caller from HMC Ambulance Service endorses a patient in a way that does not meet the re quirements of SOP 4.8.B or SOP 8.8 , as applicable, staff may politely direct the caller to provide an IMIST - AMBO endorsement. 5.4 In all cases, where a caller provides names , corporation numbers, contact information, locations or patient identifiers Clinical Desk staff must reconfirm they have understood and documented this correctly by repeating this back to the caller (closed loop communication). 6. Placing a Caller on Hold : 6.1 A caller should not be put on hold unless necessary to manage the case appropriately or resolve the query. 6.2 Where a caller is the treating clinician of a critically ill patient or is directly involved in managing a critical situation: 6.2.1 The caller should not b e put on hold. 6.2.2 The details should be taken of the caller, the patient and the situation including an IMIST - AMBO report when appropriate and ask them to keep their phone nearby for your call - back. (‘Thank you, I will follow this up now and will return your call shortly, please remain close to your phone.’) 6.2.3 Im

mediately resolve any concerns or endorse the case to the appropriate person to resolve. 6.2.4 Call the caller back to update them on their call and the actions taken, or to be taken. 6.3 When placing a caller on hold, ask if you can put them on hold, explain the reason for placing them on hold and advise them to remain on the line. 6.3.1 Use the Hold/Mute function to place the caller on hold. 6.3.2 Check in periodically with the caller when on hold if it is expected to take l onger than 1 - minute to resolve the concern. 6.3.3 Callers should not be put on hold for longer than necessary. 6.4 If unable to place a caller on hold for any reason, follow the procedure as set out at 6.2.2 above. 7. Transferring of Calls : 7.1 Calls should only be transfe rred when resolving the call cannot be achieved without transferring the call. 7.2 Always inform the caller that you need to transfer their call and to whom and request them to remain on the line. 7.3 Before transferring any call: 7.3.1 Confirm the person to which the c all will be transferred is available and ready to receive the call. 7.3.2 Confirm the caller’s name and contact number in case the call is dropped during the transfer and

you need to call them back. 8. Conferencing Calls : 8.1 When necessary, conference calls shall be conducted over the HMC Ambulance Service Cisco System. 8.2 Before conducting the conference call, staff must ensure they know all contact numbers and names of participants and are familiar with the objective of the call.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 590 8.3 International conference calls will be facilitated by the EMD: Retrievals Coordinator using the approved phone for international dialing. Local conference calls can be facilitated through any HMC Cisco phone at the Clinical Desk. 8.4 During conference calls: 8.4.1 Clinical Desk staff hosting/setting - up t he conference call should open the call by confirming all callers are online and introducing callers to each other where necessary. 8.4.2 Staff must remain attentive on the call, take adequate notes for documentation purposes and obtain all required information to prevent unnecessary follow - up/repeat calls. 8.4.3 Use the mute function when not actively talking on the call to reduce background noise disrupting other speakers on the call. 8.4.4 When engaged in a conference call, do not put the call on hold as this will put all callers on hold, use the mute function if required. 8.4.5 On completion of the conference call the staff hosting/setting - up the call should close the call, after ensuring all discussion is completed, professionally. (“Thank you all for participating in this dis cussion, I appreciate your

time. Should there be any changes or updates we will be in further contact, Goodbye.”) 9. Closing the Call : 9.1 Clinical Desk staff must end all calls professionally regardless of who the caller is. 9.2 Calls shall be closed with a standar d script or a reasonably similar appropriate script. (‘Thank you, please call us back if anything changes. Goodbye.’) 10. Clinical Desk IVR System : 10.1 Clinical Desk phones are managed with a call distribution system based on the caller’s selection for preferred service offering. 10.2 Calls will be routed based on caller selection and, where the original selection is unavailable, re - routed based on predetermined call routing priorities. 10.3 Clinical Desk staff must ensure their phone is on and working, and that t he Cisco Finesse application is logged - in throughout their shift. 10.4 Staff must be available in the Cisco Finesse System (IVR availability) at all times they are at their console. When leaving the console, or unavailable for any other reason, they must make t hemselves unavailable in accordance to 3.4 above .’

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 592 SOP 8.8 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services Clinical Desk Procedure 8.8 HOSPITAL PRE - ARR IVAL NOTIFICATION Implementation Date 01 July 2022 Purpose Establishes a standardized process and endorsement criteria for all hospital pre - arrival notifications by HMC Ambulance Service . Responsibility ALL PATIENT FACING A ND CLINICAL DESK STA FF 1. Background and Purpose : 1.1 The pre - arrival notification of hospitals for the imminent arrival of pathway or priority 1 patients is necessary to ensure optimal and timely provision of critical care to patients and is a requirement under HMC Ambulance Service Policy. 1.2 This SOP aims to standardize the approach to the pre - arrival notification of hospitals including the endorsement from patient facing clinicians to the Clinical Desk and the notification of the receiving facility by the Clinical Desk. 2. Definitions applicable within this SOP : 2.1 ‘Hospital Pre - arrival Notification’ means the intentional notification of the receiving hospital patient care unit of a patient being transported by HMC Ambulance

Service to that unit, prior to the patient’s arrival. 3. Requirements for pre - arrival notification : 3.1 It is the responsibility of the patient facing Critical Care Paramedic or Ambulance Paramedic to initiate the pre - arrival notification process by notifying the Clinical Desk as soon as reasonably possible. 3.2 Pre - arrival notification of receiving hospitals shall be conducted by any clinica l staff assigned to the Clinical Desk including the CC: Emergency, CC: Retrieval or LifeFlight Coordinator. 3.2.1 All pre - arrival notifications for LifeFlight cases should be done through the LifeFlight Coordinator unless there is a need for clinical guidance, o r the LifeFlight Coordinator is unable to assist for any reason. 3.2.2 All uncomplicated pre - arrival notifications from a treating CCP should be done through the LifeFlight Coordinator unless there is a need for clinical guidance, or the LifeFlight Coordinator i s unable to assist for any reason. 3.2.3 Any pre - arrival notification from an Ambulance Paramedic, or where the on - scene CCP requires additional clinical support beyond pre - arrival notification, should be done through the CC: Emergency. 3.2.3.1 Where the CC: E

mergency i s unable to assist for any reason the CC: Retrieval should facilitate the pre - arrival notification. 3.3 Hospital pre - arrival notification must be done, from the Clinical Desk to the hospital, through the approved hospital pre - arrival notification ‘hotlines’. 3.4 N otification of receiving facilities must be undertaken for any of the following patient groups being transported by HMC Ambulance Service: 3.4.1 Any priority 1 patient regardless of gender or age. 3.4.2 Any pathway patient including, but not limited to, Trauma Bypass, STEMI, CVA and WWRC.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 593 3.4.3 Any Interfacility Transfer in which the patient has deteriorated, or any significant changes have been made to the clinical care of the patient. 3.4.4 Any other patient group or receiving facility as indicated in SOP, CPG or reasonable requ est by management. 3.5 Notification of the receiving facility shall be undertaken prior to the arrival of the patient at the receiving unit and all available clinical information endorsed to enable the receiving unit to facilitate a safe rapid transition of ca re. 3.6 Hospital pre - arrival notification does not constitute a request for the receiving facility to accept the patient. Unless the receiving facility is on divert, the facility is mandated to accept and receive the patient from the HMC Ambulance Service. 4. St andard endorsement information and structure : 4.1 The transporting Critical Care Paramedic or Ambulance Paramedic shall use the standard patient endorsement tool, IMIST - AMBO, in accordance with SOP 4.8.B when endorsing any patient to the Clinical Desk. 4.1.1 Notification of the Clinical Desk should be through a recognized communication system including Clin

ical 1 tetra channel or the Clinical Desk phoneline (4439 8833). 4.1.2 Where LifeFlight are unable to communicate on these comm unication lines due to reception challenges in flight, they may use MS Teams for this purpose. 4.1.2.1 All notification messages on MS Teams must include all IMIST - AMBO information in a standard format. 4.1.2.2 MS Teams notification messages must be received and acknowled ged by the LifeFlight Coordinator at the Clinical Desk before the pre - arrival notification is made to the receiving hospital. 4.2 The Clinical Desk shall endorse the patient to the receiving hospital using the IMIST - AMBO approach in accordance with SOP 4.8.B providing the below minimum information for all cases. 4.2.1 Age and Gender of the patient 4.2.2 Chief Complaint or Mechanism of Injury 4.2.3 Level of Consciousness 4.2.4 Cardiorespiratory status 4.2.5 Treatment provided and ongoing 4.2.6 Expected time of arrival 4.3 This information (4.2) must be delivered using a standard IMIST - O and ETA bundle structure as a minimum for all cases, and where ‘Allergies’, ‘Medication’ or ‘Background’ are applicable to the specific case, this should also be end orsed. 5. Exceptional endorsemen

t information: 5.1 Pre - arrival hospital notification should be done for all patients going to Heart Hospital, regardless of not meeting pre - arrival notification criteria set out in 3.4 above. 5.2 Pre - arrival hospital notification of Si dra Hospital for any patient being transported by LifeFlight must include clear notification that the patient is arriving by helicopter. 6. Pre - arrival notification process ( Appendix 8.8.1 ):

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 594 6.1 The transporting Critic al Care Paramedic or Ambulance Paramedic shall notify the Clinical Desk of all patients requiring pre - arrival notification within a reasonable timeframe to enable pre - arrival notification. 6.2 The CC: Emergency, CC: Retrieval or LifeFlight Coordinator shall re ceive and document the information per 4.3 above and any additional relevant information endorsed from the transporting unit using the approved documentation standard as stipulated by management. 6.3 The CC: Emergency, CC: Retrieval or LifeFlight Coordinator s hall endorse the patient to the receiving hospital in a timely manner whilst adhering to set pre - arrival notification standards. 6.3.1 All hospital pre - arrival notification calls must be opened professionally with introduction of the caller’s name and reason for calling. (“ Good day, this is [your name] of the Clinical Desk, HMC Ambulance Services, may I endorse a patient?.” 6.3.2 The caller shall confirm they are speaking to the correct receiving facility and unit for all calls before patient endorsement. 6.3.3 The caller sh all request and document the receiving person

’s name and corporation number and note the time of the call. 6.3.4 The caller shall provide the relevant patient and clinical information in accordance with 4.2 and 4.3 above, ensuring a minimum of “IMIST - O & ETA” to the receiving facility. 6.3.5 The caller should advise the receiving facility that the estimated time of arrival is an estimation and is dependent on traffic and road conditions. 6.3.6 The caller shall close the call professionally, document the notification and case information appropriately and, where necessary, advise the transporting unit of any further updates. 6.4 The caller shall advise the receiving or transporting unit of any changes to the patient's disposition, treatment or estimated time of arrival should this change after notification has been provided. 6.5 Notwithstanding 6.3.1 above, the management of telephone calls for the purposes of hospital pre - arrival notification should adhere to the provisions of SOP 8.7 . 7. Hospital re fusal of patients during pre - arrival notification : 7.1 During pre - arrival notification of the preferred receiving facility, the doctor or nurse may refuse to accept the patient in their unit, referring the patient to an

other unit or hospital. Reasons for this may range from bed availability to triage concerns and are not within HMCAS control. 7.2 Where a pathway patient (STEMI, CVA, Trauma Bypass, WWRC ) is being refused at a designated receiving facility or unit: 7.2.1 The Clinical Coordinator or LifeFlight Coordinator doing the notification should advise the unit that the patient meets pathway criteria. 7.2.2 If they continue to refuse or refer the patient, their refusal or referral should be reconfirmed and documented as a refusal in the CC Clinical Shift Report with the Corporation number of the doctor/nurse. 7.2.3 The new receiving hospital or unit should be pre - notified as clinically appropriate. 7.2.4 The Clinical Coordinator or LifeFlight Coordinator handling the case must update the transporting unit of the new disposit ion. 7.3 Where the patient being referred to an alternative facility or unit does not meet pathway criteria :

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 595 7.3.1 The refusal or referral should be reconfirmed and annotated in “Other” (notes section of the case) on the CC Clinical Shift Report with the Corporation number of the doctor/nurse. 7.3.2 The case should be reported under the relevant pathology classification/section in the CC Clinical Shift Report (i.e. do not select refusal). 7.3.3 The new receiving hospital or unit should be pre - notified as clinically appropriate. 7.3.4 The Clinical Coordinator or LifeFlight Coordinator handling the case must update the transporting unit of the new disposition .

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 596 SOP 8.9 Hamad Medical Corporation Ambulance Service Standard Operating Procedures Sector HMC Ambulance Services Clinical Desk Procedure 8 . 9 TRANSFER ESCALATION TO THE CLINICAL D ESK Implementation Date 01 J une 20 22 Purpose To set out the standard procedure for the escalation of transfers to the Clinical Desk from HCC and NCC with the intention to improve standardization and consistency in quality whilst mitigating unnecessary delays . Responsibility ALL STAFF ASSIGNED T O THE CLINICAL DESK, NCC AND HCC 1. Background and Purpose: 1.1 To facilitate safe patient transport by ambulance between healthcare facilities, high risk transfer cases need to be evaluated and triaged prior to dispatch of resources; thereby, ensuring that the patient receives the right level of care and transfer platform at the right time. 2. Definitions: 2.1 'Clinical Privileging’ refers to the authorization levels determining what medical treatments HMCAS staff may provide based on their area of operations and clinical exposure as set out in the HMCAS Clinical Practice Guidelines. 2.2 ‘HMCAS service offering’ refers to operational divisions wit

hin HMCAS that provides patient transfer including Charlie, LifeFlight, Retrieval, Interfacility Transfer (IFT) and Alpha . 2.3 ‘Immediate Transfer’ means an inter - facility transfer of a pathway patient or any patient requirin g an emergent intervention immediately on arrival at the receiving facility such as STEMI, Stroke, meeting Trauma Bypass Criteria, rescue PCI, immediate neurological intervention. Formerly known as "push, urgent, time critical" transfers . 2.4 ‘Level of Care’ m eans the level of clinical care required to safely transfer a patient which is determined by the clinical condition and ongoing treatment needs of the patient, and clinical privileging levels of clinicians within HMCAS . 2.5 ‘Scheduled Transfer’ means an inter - facility transfer of a stable patient for bed management, step - down care or for further clinical investigation and monitoring in the receiving facility with a negotiated pick - up time scheduled based on available resources . 2.5.1 ‘Scheduled Urgent Transfer’ means a scheduled inter - facility transfer of a stable patient that does not meet pathway criteria or require immediate limb or lifesaving treatment at the receiving facility; however

, requires an upgrade in their level of clinical care within the following 2 - ho urs to prevent clinical harm or deterioration. 2.6 ‘Triage ’ means the process of weighing the relevant available clinical and logistic information to decide on the appropriate urgency of transfer, level of care, appropriate HMCAS service offering and patient s tability for transfer. 3. General Principles: 3.1 The purpose of escalation to the Clinical Desk is primarily for the confirmation of level of care where this is anticipated to exceed the clinical privileging of an Ambulance Paramedic. 3.2 Escalation of cases to the Clinical Desk should be closely monitored to prevent unreasonable delays in dispatch, especially where the patient is suspected to require immediate transfer. To minimize any delays case ownership is defined below, where the case owner remains responsible to track escalation and coordination progress until resources are dispatched . 3.2.1 Calls received at the HCC will remain under HCC (UTL) oversight and coordination (ownership) until escalated to the Clinical Desk.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 597 3.2.2 Calls received at the NCC will remain under NCC (DTL) oversight and coordination (ownership) until escalated to the Clinical Desk. 3.2.3 Cases escalated to the Clinical Desk will remain under the oversight and coordination (ownership) of the Clinical Desk (EMD: Retrievals Coordinator) until endorsed back to the HCC/NCC. 4. Criteria for interfacility transfer escalation to the Clinical Desk: 4.1 All interfacility transfer cases meeting the following criteria should be escalated to the Clinical Desk: 4.1.1 Any Protocol 45 or Protocol 46 case that is indicated by ProQA for e scalation. 4.1.2 Any patient for which the referring facility requests a LifeFlight or helicopter response . 4.1.3 Any patient for which the referring facility requests a level of care that is higher than an Ambulance Paramedic (CCP, Doctor or other specialized transpo rt team). 4.1.4 Any patient for which the referring facility requests specialized equipment for the transfer other than oxygen, ECG/heart monitor or suction. 4.1.5 Any patient identified with clinical ‘attachments’ or ‘drains’ beyond the clinical privileging (scope of practice) o

f an Ambulance Paramedic . 4.1.6 Where the call - taker/dispatcher has specific and relevant clinical concerns about the patient’s clinical stability or level - of - care needs. 5. NCC Escalation process: 5.1 Cases identified for escalation to the Clinical Desk ma y be escalated directly to an available Clinical Coordinator at the Clinical Desk in NCC. 5.2 The call - taker/dispatcher escalating the case to the Clinical Desk must provide: 5.2.1 NJM CFS number 5.2.2 Patient identification in the form of a name, HC#, QID# or passport # . 5.2.3 Referral facility call - back contact number 5.2.4 Outline of the case including referring and receiving facility, and the chief complaint . 5.2.5 Why the case is being escalated (the escalation trigger) 5.3 At the time of escalation, the call - taker/dispatcher es calating the case must add a CFS note indicating who the case was escalated to and why it was escalated. 5.4 Once the case has been triaged, the Clinical Coordinator must take necessary actions to update the DTL of the triage level of care, preferred HMCAS ser vice offering and priority. 5.4.1 If the case is triaged for immediate CCP transport the CC should update the DTL and advise t

he appropriate CCP resource reqiured (Charlie/LifeFlight/Retrieval) and, where possible, the CC should update the responding CCP of the known information about the case. 5.4.2 If the case is triaged for scheduled CCP transport, the CC should update the DTL and advise for the case to be handed over to EMD: Retrieval Coordinators to manage further following standard scheduled transfer procedures. 5.4.3 If the case is triaged for Ambulance Paramedic transport, the CC must update the DTL accordingly.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 598 6. HCC Escalation process: 6.1 Cases identified for escalation to the Clinical Desk must be escalated directly to the EMD: Retrievals Coordinator seated at the Clin ical Desk in NCC. 6.2 The call - taker/dispatcher escalating the case to the Clinical Desk must provide: 6.2.1 Valencia CAD trip number 6.2.2 Outline of the case including referring and receiving facility, and the chief complaint . 6.2.3 Why the case is being escalated (the escala tion trigger) 6.3 Any escalation to the Clinical Desk must be documented in the relevant CAD notes: 6.3.1 The call - taker/dispatcher escalating the case must add a Valencia CAD note, using note type “CD - Escalation”, at the time of escalation noting “ Escalated to [nam e of person contacted ]”. 6.3.2 On receiving any escalation from the HCC, the EMD: Retrievals Coordinator must open the case in CAD and add a note “ Received from [name of escalating person ]”, using note type “CD - Escalation”. 6.4 The call - taker/dispatcher escalating t he case should hot transfer the caller to the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator who shall: 6.4.1 Reconfirm the patient’s name and ID from the Valenci

a CAD. 6.4.2 Where the caller is a PHCC , The EMD: Retrievals Coordinator must obtain additional specific information as set out in the “PHCC Escalation Additional Questions” (see Appendix 8.9.C ) and endorse the patient information to the Clinical Coordinator for them to advise on requirement to immediately dispatch a CCP unit. 6.4.3 Where the caller is an HMC hospital , the EMD: Retrievals Coordinator should directly endorse the case to a Clinical Coordinator and, if possible, hot transfer the caller to t he Clinical Coordinator for direct clinical interrogation. 6.5 Once the case has been triaged, the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator must take necessary actions to, where appropriate, arrange for a CCP unit from the NCC DTL and update the escalating call - taker/dispat cher. 6.5.1 If the case is triaged for Ambulance Paramedic transport, the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator must update the call - taker/dispatcher that escalated the case and, at the same time, add a note in CAD using the note type “CD - Closed”. e.g.: “ Endorsed back to [ NAME ].” 6.5.2 If the case is triaged for immediate CCP transport, the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator must liaise with the DTL and, if LifeFlight is required th

e LifeFlight Coordinator, to get a CCP unit and update the call - taker/dispatcher of the CCP requirement. 6.5.2.1 On ce the CCP has changed onto the appropriate Tetra Radio channel the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator should add a note in CAD using the note type “CD - Closed”. e.g.: “CCP arranged, endorsed back to [NAME].” 6.5.3 If the case is triaged for scheduled CCP transport , the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator must update the call - taker/dispatcher that escalated the case and, at the same time, add a note in CAD using the note type “CD - Closed”. e.g.: “Endorsed back to [NAME].” 6.5.3.1 Any case triaged for scheduled CCP/Doctor transfer must be handed over to the EMD: Retrieval Coordinator to manage further.

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 599 7. Documentation and reporting: 7.1 Any case escalated to the Clinical Desk must be appropriately documented in the relevant NJM or Valencia System at the time of escalation. Additional notes with further clinical or operational details may be added as usual in the case notes. 7.2 Once feedback is provided following triage, a clear note must be added in the relevant NJM or Valencia System at the time of the feedback indicating the triage decision. 7.3 The C linical Coordinator should ensure that full and adequately detailed clinical notes are added to the NJM or Valencia CAD system documenting the clinical screening findings, consultations and triage decision. 7.3.1 Where the escalated case is a Health Centre case, the Clinical Coordinator is not required to add notes unless they have additional information not already reflected in the ProQA or case details. 7.4 EMD: Retrieval Coordinators should ensure that all CCP, doctor or team led transfers are reported in the Retr ieval Activity Shift Report. 8. Appendices : 8.1 Appendix 8.9.C: PHCC Escalation Additional Questions

Return to Table of Contents Version 4.4 – November 2024 Page 627 A PPENDIX 4.4.D U NIT A VAILABILITY E SCALATION P ROCEDURES Escalation Procedures AS 9032, 6.5 Actions Unit Availability 30 % ➢ NCC OSCAR and OSCAR Command (jointly) to check the status of units delayed on scene or delayed at destination that can be expedited. ➢ Update Silver Command. ➢ Cease P2 IFT from PHCC and Private facilities and utilize SAS Resources Foxtrot units - priority units for being reassigned A1.11 (after 1500 hours) and A8.3. Escalation to remain in place until a minimum of 30 minutes sustained unit availability >30% Unit Availability 25 % ➢ Recall NCC staff to the NCC/HCC - ceasing breaks for NCC/HCC staff. Unit Availability 20 % ➢ All Actions as per unit availability of 30% plus: ➢ Announce low coverage as general broadcast on t he Tetra radio (work with Distribution Team Leaders). ➢ OSCAR NCC to brief call - takers to ensure ‘refer low acuity patients’ as per PROQA protocol is optimized. ➢ Deploy Production OSCAR to spoke points - respond to P1 calls. ➢ Deploy Production Supervisor to spoke points - respond to P1 calls (unless within 60 minutes of end of shift). ➢ Oscar Command to instigate Cohorti

ng at HGH and expedited handover at other units. ➢ OSCAR access to report to the ED with the ‘greatest pressure’ to support turn - ar ound. ➢ Check the status of units responding to P2 cases that can be diverted to P1 cases (work with Clinical Desk). ➢ Extend EAS shifts (work with Production Team Leader). ➢ Extend NCC and HCC shifts. ➢ STOP all Interfaculty Transfers (unless critical transfer – clinical desk to re - triage). ➢ Review lower acuity patient transports from Facility to Home and vice versa QRI Physiotherapy, Hydrotherapy, Dentistry, Dermatology, and out - on - pass. ➢ Protocol 33 calls to be re - triaging by clinical desk and potential stood down . ➢ Use of A2.11. Escalation to remain in place until a minimum of 30 minutes sustained unit availability >30% Unit Availability 15 % ➢ All Actions as per unit availability of 30% and 20% plus: ➢ Inform Gold Command. ➢ Silver Communication to attend NCC. ➢ Stop all PTS call except dialysis cases. Escalation to remain in place until a minimum of 30 minutes sustained unit availability >30% Unit Availability 10 % ➢ All Actions as per unit availability of 30%, 20% and 15% plus: ➢ Rural ambulances to be redeployed ‘closure’ to urban

area as per Silver Command. ➢ Silver Clinical to report to 1) ED to support turn - around of 2) NCC to support clinical desk . Escalation to remain in place until a minimum of 30 minutes sustained unit availability >30%

A TRAUMA BYPASS CRITE RIA TRAUMA BYPASS CRITERIA ASSESS MECHANISM OF INJURY Falls • Adult fall: 6 meters (20ft or 2 stories) or higher • Child fall: 3 meters or higher (10ft or 1 story) or twice patient’s height • Fall from horse, camel or similar type of animal Motor vehicle crash with: • Traumatic death in the same vehicle (except bus or mini - bus type of vehicle) • Significant intrusion >30cm into the occupant compartment or >45cm at any site • Ejection (partial or complete) from vehicle (including ATV - type vehicle) • Person trapped under vehicle and/ or extrication >20min • Rollover with roof deformity >40cm Motorcycle accident with speed >30km/h Pedestrian/ paediatric/ bicyclist struck by vehicle (thrown/ run over/ or with impact with speed >20km/h) Burns with evidence of trauma OR >20% BSA in adults OR >10% BSA in children OR any significant neck/ facial burns with potential airway compromise. Hanging or near - hanging with evidence of trauma (eg. hanging fall greater than height of patient or obvious spine deformity). Drowning or near - drowning with evidence of trauma (eg. di ving into shallo

w water). If ONE or MORE of the listed criteria are met, you should immediately notify the CTL and transport patients DIRECTLY to HGH Trauma Resuscitation Unit, effectively bypassing the HGH RIAMS room/ Bay 1; and/ or bypassing other closer hospitals. If NONE of the listed criteria are met, transport patient to closest hospital emergency department. Any deviation from these criteria MUST be authorized through CTL. ASSESS VITAL SIGNS AND LEVEL OF CONSCIOUSNESS • Glasgow Coma Scale <15 (consider effects of alcohol or drugs) • Sustained systolic blood pressure: • <90 in adults • <age - specific normal value in children • <110 if age >65 years. • Respiratory Rate: • <10 OR >29 in adults • infant to 1 year old <20 • any patient needing respiratory assistance ASSESS ANATOMIC CRITERIA (PATTERN OF INJURY) • Chest wall instability or deformity (eg. flail chest) • Traumatic amputation above wrist or ankle • Penetrating trauma to head, neck, torso or extremities above elbow or knee • Open and/ or depressed skull fracture • Suspected pelvic fracture/ unstable pelvis • Neurological deficit in patients with spinal trauma • Two or more fractures of either the humerus or femur • Crushed, de - glov

ed, mangled or pulseless extremity ASSESS SPECIAL PATIENT OR SYSTEM CONSIDERATION Patients who have sustained significant trauma but do not fit any of the criteria above but are: • Older patients (>65 years) • Pregnant (>20 weeks) Is there a reasonable clinical concern despite clearing all of the above criteria AND the CTL is in agreement?
